text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Context management (automatic)

To keep your context small and your runs cheap, the harness automatically
elides the *raw text* of large `read`/`bash` outputs after they have been on
screen for a few turns, replacing each with a short `[context-saver: …]` stub
that names the file or command. The document was fully read when it was
current — nothing was hidden from your initial pass — only the bulk is dropped
from later turns. Two habits keep this lossless:

- As you read a document, **record the specific facts, figures, dates, and
  verbatim clauses you will need** into your notes or draft. Don't rely on the
  full text staying resident.
- If you later need the exact text of a doc that now shows a `[context-saver:
  …]` stub, simply **`read` that file again** — the full content returns.

## File-format skills

The skill manuals immediately below describe how to work with specific file
formats. Read them before tackling the task.


## Skill: docx

---
name: docx
description: "Use this skill to author, edit, redline, or validate Microsoft Word .docx files. Covers creating new documents from markdown or templates, editing existing documents in place, generating tracked-changes redlines, adding comments, and accepting/rejecting revisions. For READING existing .docx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'draft a memo', 'mark up the agreement', 'redline this', 'add comments to', 'fill the engagement letter template'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .xlsx, .pptx, or .doc (legacy Word)."
---

# DOCX authoring, editing, redlining

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .docx, use the harness `read` tool. It already returns structured text via pandoc. This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *validating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a new doc from markdown | `scripts/generate_from_md.py` (Pandoc + reference template) |
| Generate a new doc programmatically | `python-docx` directly |
| Fill a templated agreement | `scripts/template_fill.py` (docxtpl / Jinja) |
| Edit an existing doc | `scripts/unpack.py` → mutate XML → `scripts/pack.py` |
| Produce a tracked-changes redline | `scripts/redline.py` |
| Add comments to a passage | `scripts/comments_add.py` |
| Accept all redlines | `scripts/accept_changes.py` |
| Validate a docx before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` (mandatory final step) |

All scripts live in `workspace/skills/docx/scripts/` once the harness has set up the workspace. Invoke them via `bash`.

## Creating a new document

Pick by what you have:

- **Markdown content + a styled firm template** → `generate_from_md.py input.md out.docx template.docx`. Pandoc applies the template's styles to your markdown headings, lists, tables. Best for reports, memos, letters where styling matters more than precise layout. Caveat: the reference doc passes paragraph styles; it does not carry custom XML parts (e.g., comment threads).
- **A template with named placeholders** → `template_fill.py template.docx context.json out.docx`. docxtpl renders Jinja2 expressions inside the template. Best for engagement letters, NDAs, structured agreements.
- **Programmatic build** → write Python using `python-docx`. Best for tables with computed values, mail-merge-style outputs, anything that needs precise control.

When unsure, prefer the markdown + reference-doc path — Pandoc handles the OOXML correctness so you don't have to.

## Editing an existing document

Three-step pattern:

```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.docx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/word/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.docx
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Key files inside the unpacked tree:

- `word/document.xml` — body content (paragraphs, runs, tables)
- `word/styles.xml` — paragraph + run style definitions
- `word/numbering.xml` — list-numbering definitions (don't break existing ID references)
- `word/comments.xml` — comment thread content (created by `comments_add.py`)
- `word/header*.xml`, `word/footer*.xml` — running headers/footers
- `[Content_Types].xml` — MIME registration for every part. Edit when you add a new part type.
- `_rels/` and `word/_rels/` — relationships between parts. Edit when you reference a new image, comment, or external resource.

### Run-merging gotcha

Word writes adjacent runs (`<w:r>`) with identical formatting separately. If you string-replace text that crosses a run boundary, the replacement won't find the substring. `unpack.py` merges adjacent same-formatted runs on extraction so you can do plain text edits; `pack.py` is permissive about whatever run structure you write back.

### Smart-quote escaping

Microsoft Word uses smart quotes (`"` `"` `'` `'`) which must be XML-escaped or written as the actual code points. `unpack.py` substitutes them with XML entities for editing safety; `pack.py` reverses the substitution before zipping. Don't manually re-escape — let the scripts handle it.

### Whitespace preservation

Trailing whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements is significant. Both `unpack.py` and `pack.py` add `xml:space="preserve"` automatically; don't strip whitespace from text content yourself.

## Redlines (tracked changes)

```bash
python scripts/redline.py original.docx revised.docx redlined.docx \
  --author "Reviewer" --date "2026-04-30"
```

Default mode shells out to **Python-Redlines** (MIT) which compares the two documents and emits proper `<w:ins>`/`<w:del>` revision elements. Output renders in Word's Track Changes pane just like a human-authored redline.

### Manual mode

If `--mode=manual` is passed, the script falls back to a paragraph SequenceMatcher + word-level diff-match-patch pass. Use this when:
- Python-Redlines fails on a particular doc structure (rare).
- You want to control which paragraphs are diffed.
- The change is purely formatting (`<w:rPrChange>` runs) rather than text.

### What gets tracked

- Insertions and deletions of text
- Insertions and deletions of paragraphs (deleted-whole-paragraph case requires `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>` or you get an empty paragraph after acceptance)
- Run-property changes via `<w:rPrChange>` (formatting-only revisions)

### What doesn't get tracked

- Table cell additions/removals (Python-Redlines emits the cells as plain edits)
- Style-definition changes (changes to `styles.xml` aren't revision-trackable)
- Image swaps

## Comments

```bash
python scripts/comments_add.py document.docx comments.json
```

`comments.json` is a list of `{anchor_text, author, comment}` objects. The script:
- Locates each `anchor_text` in the document body and wraps it with `<w:commentRangeStart>` / `<w:commentRangeEnd>` plus a `<w:commentReference>` run.
- Creates or appends to `word/comments.xml` (with proper id assignment).
- Patches `[Content_Types].xml` and `word/_rels/document.xml.rels` if commenting is being added for the first time.

Anchor matching is exact-string. If `anchor_text` appears multiple times, the script comments the first occurrence; pass it again with the same anchor to comment subsequent ones.

`<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` must be siblings of the `<w:r>` runs they bracket — never nested inside a run. If you write comments by hand, follow this rule.

## Accept / reject changes

```bash
python scripts/accept_changes.py redlined.docx accepted.docx
```

Uses LibreOffice headless via a documented StarBasic macro:

```basic
Sub AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.store() End Sub
```

LibreOffice must be installed and on PATH (`soffice` binary). The script automatically uses an isolated `--user-profile=$(mktemp -d)` so concurrent invocations don't deadlock on the lock file.

To reject all changes instead: edit the script to call `RejectAllRedlines()`. To accept selectively: this isn't supported — open the doc in Word.

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring the task complete.**

```bash
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Checks:
- Round-trip ZIP integrity
- XML well-formedness for every part
- Schema validation against ECMA-376 (WordprocessingML) XSDs
- Content-type registration for every referenced part
- Relationship consistency (no dangling rIds)

Exit code 0 = valid. Non-zero exit code with line-number diagnostics = fix and re-pack.

## Common pitfalls

- **Legacy `.doc` (binary) is not supported.** Convert with `soffice --convert-to docx input.doc` first.
- **List numbering breaks after edits.** Numbering lives in `word/numbering.xml` keyed by `numId`. If you delete a list, also delete its numId reference; if you reorder, don't change IDs.
- **Headers and footers are separate parts** (`word/header1.xml`, etc.). Edits to body content don't touch them.
- **Pandoc reference-doc passes paragraph styles only.** Custom XML parts (comments, tracked changes baseline) are not carried over.
- **Don't pretty-print whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements.** Pretty-printing breaks runs that depend on exact spacing.
- **Tables: dual width specs.** Each cell needs both `columnWidths` (in `<w:tblGrid>`) and per-cell `<w:tcW w:type="dxa">`. Percentages (`pct`) render fine in Word but break in Google Docs.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- Producing PDFs from .docx: pipe through `soffice --convert-to pdf` after this skill is done.
- Signing / encryption / DRM.
- Word macros (`.docm` with VBA).



## Skill: pptx

---
name: pptx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft PowerPoint .pptx files. Covers generating decks from scratch (HTML+PptxGenJS, Marp markdown-to-slides, or python-pptx), editing existing decks in place, and validating output. For READING existing .pptx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a deck', 'create slides', 'edit slide N', 'add a chart slide'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .xlsx, or .ppt (legacy)."
---

# PPTX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .pptx, use the harness `read` tool (markitdown extracts slide text). This skill is for *writing* and *editing*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a deck from scratch (HTML/CSS) | `scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js` |
| Generate a deck from markdown | `scripts/generate_marp.sh` |
| Build slides programmatically | `python-pptx` directly |
| Edit a shape on an existing slide | `scripts/edit_shape.py` (JSON patch) |
| Add or remove a slide | unpack → edit → pack |
| QA a deck deterministically | `scripts/deterministic_qa.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Generation modalities

**HTML/CSS via PptxGenJS** (preferred for visual fidelity):
```bash
node scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js deck.json out.pptx
```
`deck.json` describes slides as a JSON tree; the script invokes PptxGenJS (and html2pptx for HTML inputs) to produce a fully-editable .pptx. Best for branded decks with gradients, custom fonts, complex shapes.

**Markdown via Marp**:
```bash
bash scripts/generate_marp.sh deck.md out.pptx
```
Best for content-heavy decks (lectures, reports) where markdown is more natural than JSON.

**Programmatic via python-pptx**:
Best when shapes are computed (e.g., one slide per data row). Requires manual EMU positioning.

## Editing existing decks

Three-step pattern, like docx:
```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.pptx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/ppt/slides/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.pptx
python scripts/validate.py output.pptx
```

For surgical shape edits without unpacking, use `edit_shape.py`:
```bash
python scripts/edit_shape.py input.pptx \
  --slide 2 --shape "Title 1" --op set_text --value "New title"
```

JSON patch ops: `set_text`, `set_position` (EMU), `set_size`, `recolor`, `delete`.

## OOXML gotchas specific to pptx

- **Use `defusedxml.minidom`, NOT `xml.etree.ElementTree`.** ElementTree corrupts presentation namespaces during round-tripping. `unpack.py` and `pack.py` use minidom; if you write your own XML manipulation, do the same.
- **EMU units everywhere.** 1 inch = 914400 EMU. Slide positions, sizes, font sizes (in pt × 100) all use derived EMU values.
- **Placeholders vs free shapes.** Placeholders inherit from slide masters; free shapes don't. Editing a placeholder's text is `<a:t>` content; editing its layout requires master-slide changes.
- **Don't pretty-print pptx XML on pack.** Whitespace-significant runs (`<a:r>`) break if reformatted. `pack.py` preserves the original whitespace.
- **Slide cloning is more than a file copy.** Use `unpack` + `pack` — manual file copies miss the rIds in `_rels/` and the `Content_Types.xml` registration.

## Deterministic QA loop

After every generation, run:

```bash
python scripts/thumbnail.py deck.pptx thumbs/   # PDF + JPEGs per slide
python scripts/deterministic_qa.py deck.pptx > qa.json
```

`deterministic_qa.py` checks:
- Shape bounding boxes don't extend past slide edges
- No two shapes overlap with > 50% area intersection
- Font sizes ≥ 11pt for body text, ≥ 18pt for titles
- All placeholders are filled (no `Click to add title` defaults)
- Bullet lists don't exceed 7 items per slide

Output is JSON listing each violation with slide number and shape id. Fix violations and re-render.

(A vision-model QA pass is intentionally out of scope for v1. Add `--use-vision` later if deterministic checks miss layout issues.)

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring done.** Schema-validates against ECMA-376 PresentationML XSDs, checks rId consistency, content-type registration.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- SmartArt creation (limited python-pptx support).
- Complex embedded charts beyond what python-pptx exposes.
- Slide transitions / animations (rarely matter for legal output).
- Vision-model layout review (deferred to v2).



## Skill: xlsx

---
name: xlsx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft Excel .xlsx files. Covers building workbooks with formulas, editing existing files, recalculating formulas, and scanning for #REF!/#DIV/0!/#VALUE! errors. For READING existing .xlsx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a model', 'create a spreadsheet', 'fill the schedule', 'recalculate'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .pptx, or .xls (legacy Excel)."
---

# XLSX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .xlsx, use the harness `read` tool (pandas extracts every sheet as a markdown table). This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *recalculating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Build a workbook from scratch | `openpyxl` directly, or `scripts/build_workbook.py` for banker conventions |
| Edit cells in an existing file | `openpyxl.load_workbook(...)` → mutate → save |
| Recalculate formulas (full fidelity) | `scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py` |
| Recalculate formulas (no LibreOffice) | `scripts/recalc_pure_python.py` |
| Scan for formula errors | `scripts/scan_errors.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Banker conventions (mandatory for financial models)

Apply these to every workbook unless the task explicitly overrides:

- **Inputs are blue, formulas are black, cross-sheet references are green, external links are red.** Use `Font(color='0000FF')` etc.
- **Negatives in parentheses, not minus signs.** Use number format `#,##0;(#,##0)`.
- **Red negatives in P&L tables.** Use `#,##0;[Red](#,##0)`.
- **Accounting format for currency.** `_-* #,##0_-;-* #,##0_-;_-* "-"_-;_-@_-` (or the localized equivalent).
- **Multiples shown as `0.0x`**, not `0.0` followed by an "x" character. Format: `0.0"x"`.
- **Underline-only on totals**, not bold-and-underline. Use `Border(bottom=Side(style='thin'))`.
- **No merged cells in input ranges.** Merged cells break formulas that reference them; reserve merging for headers and titles only.
- **Units in adjacent cells**, not in the cell with the value. `($M)` next to the value, not `"$1,234M"` as a string.

`scripts/build_workbook.py` applies these conventions automatically given a JSON spec.

## Formula authoring

- **Always emit formulas, never calculated values.** If the user wants `revenue × growth`, write `=B2*C2`, not `1234.56`. The recalc step materializes values.
- **Use named ranges** for cross-sheet inputs. `wb.defined_names["assumptions"] = DefinedName(...)`. Easier to audit.
- **Document units in adjacent cells** so the model is self-explanatory.
- **No volatile functions in hot paths.** `OFFSET`, `INDIRECT`, `NOW`, `TODAY` recalculate on every change and slow large workbooks.

## Recalculation — choose your engine

`openpyxl` writes formula *strings*; it does not evaluate them. You must recalculate before delivery, otherwise consumers will see `=B2*C2` literal text where they expect numbers (in some readers) or stale cached values (in others).

**LibreOffice path** (`recalc_libreoffice.py`) — ground truth:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Drives LibreOffice headless via the StarBasic macro `ThisComponent.calculateAll(); ThisComponent.store()`. Slow (~5–10s per workbook) but matches Excel for nearly every function. Use this when the workbook contains modern Excel features.

**Pure-Python path** (`recalc_pure_python.py`) — fast, partial:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_pure_python.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Uses `xlcalculator` to evaluate every formula in pure Python. Fast (~0.5s per workbook). Covers ~80% of common functions: arithmetic, `SUM`, `IF`, `VLOOKUP`, `INDEX`/`MATCH`, basic string/date functions.

**Does NOT support**: `XLOOKUP`, `LET`, dynamic arrays (`FILTER`, `SEQUENCE`, `UNIQUE`), `LAMBDA`, `BYROW`, `TEXTJOIN` with refs, structured table references, most modern (post-2019) Excel features.

If you used any of those, run the LibreOffice path. The pure-Python path is for CI environments without LibreOffice.

## Error scan

After every recalc, scan for formula errors:

```bash
python scripts/scan_errors.py output.xlsx > errors.json
```

Reports every cell whose computed value matches `#REF!`, `#DIV/0!`, `#VALUE!`, `#NAME?`, `#NULL!`, `#NUM!`, or `#N/A`. Output is JSONL with `{sheet, address, value}` per line.

If errors exist, fix and re-recalc. Don't ship a workbook with `#REF!`s — it's the most common reason a deliverable fails QA.

## Validation gate

`scripts/validate.py output.xlsx` schema-validates against ECMA-376 SpreadsheetML XSDs and confirms ZIP integrity, content-type registration, sheet relationships.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- **PivotTables** — `openpyxl` round-trips existing pivots but cannot create or modify them. If a task requires pivot creation, escalate (Windows-only via COM, not portable).
- **DAX measures and Power Pivot** — `openpyxl` can't write these. Same limitation.
- **VBA macros (`.xlsm`)** — out of scope for this skill. Macros require Excel runtime.
- **Conditional formatting beyond simple cell-value rules** — `openpyxl` supports basic CF; complex rules (top-N, data bars across sheets) often fail to round-trip.
- **Charts beyond the python-pptx-equivalent set** — line/bar/scatter work; combo charts and trendlines round-trip unreliably.

Prepare a settlement statement for the attached commercial property closing, with full proration schedules and a reconciliation summary, plus a notes memo flagging discrepancies and assumptions. Output: `settlement-statement.docx` and `settlement-statement-notes.docx`.
I'll start by exploring the workspace to find the attached documents related to the commercial property closing.

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**A.** Seller is the owner of certain real property commonly known as 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604, more particularly described in Exhibit A attached hereto and made a part hereof (the \"Property\").

**B.** The Property consists of a mixed-use building containing approximately 18,400 square feet of total building area, situated on approximately 0.38 acres of land, comprising a ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet and twelve (12) residential apartment units (Units 2A through 2F on the second floor and Units 3A through 3F on the third floor). The Property is located within the MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District) zoning classification of the City of Bridgeport. The building was originally constructed in 1998 and underwent a partial renovation in 2019.

**C.** The Property is currently subject to a commercial lease with Coastal Provisions Market LLC (the \"Commercial Tenant\") for the ground-floor commercial space, and various residential leases with tenants occupying the residential units on the second and third floors. The Property is presently managed by Bayshore Management Co. (the \"Property Manager\") under a management agreement with the Estate.

**D.** Seller desires to sell, and Buyer desires to purchase, the Property on the terms and conditions set forth herein.

**E.** Seller has the authority to convey the Property pursuant to the fiduciary powers granted under the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford and the orders of the Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE 2 --- SALE AND PURCHASE]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Agreement to Sell and Purchase**

Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, Seller agrees to sell, transfer, and convey to Buyer, and Buyer agrees to purchase from Seller, the Property for the Purchase Price. The obligations of each party to consummate the transaction contemplated herein are subject to the satisfaction or waiver of the conditions set forth in Article 8 of this Agreement.

**Section 2.2 --- Property Description**

The Property is located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604, County of Fairfield, State of Connecticut. The Property is legally described as Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records. The sale shall include all buildings, improvements, fixtures, and appurtenances located on, attached to, or used in connection with the Property. The Property is more fully described in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference.

**Section 2.3 --- Included in Sale**

The sale of the Property shall include all of Seller\'s right, title, and interest in and to the following:

> \(a\) The land and all improvements thereon, including the mixed-use building and all related structures, parking areas, landscaping, and site improvements;
>
> \(b\) All fixtures and building systems, including without limitation heating, ventilation, air conditioning, plumbing, electrical, fire safety, and security systems;
>
> \(c\) The Commercial Lease and all Residential Leases, together with all security deposits held by Seller or the Property Manager thereunder;
>
> \(d\) All assignable permits, licenses, certificates of occupancy, warranties, and guaranties relating to the Property or any improvements thereon; and
>
> \(e\) The trade name \"4280 Harborview,\" if any, and any goodwill associated therewith.

**[ARTICLE 3 --- PURCHASE PRICE AND EARNEST MONEY]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Purchase Price**

The Purchase Price for the Property shall be Three Million Nine Hundred Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$3,900,000.00), payable as follows:

> \(a\) Earnest Money deposits as set forth in Section 3.2 of this Agreement;
>
> \(b\) Buyer\'s mortgage loan proceeds from Tidewater Savings Bank in the approximate amount of Two Million Six Hundred Forty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$2,640,000.00); and
>
> \(c\) The balance of the Purchase Price, as adjusted for prorations, credits, and other adjustments set forth herein, in immediately available funds at Closing.

**Section 3.2 --- Earnest Money Deposits**

> \(a\) **First Deposit.** Within two (2) business days of the execution of this Agreement, Buyer shall deposit the sum of One Hundred Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$100,000.00) with the Escrow Agent (Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC) as earnest money (the \"First Deposit\"). The First Deposit was deposited on April 24, 2025.
>
> \(b\) **Second Deposit.** Upon the expiration of the Due Diligence Period, and provided Buyer has not exercised its right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Article 4, Buyer shall deposit an additional Ninety-Five Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$95,000.00) with the Escrow Agent (the \"Second Deposit\"). The Second Deposit was deposited on May 22, 2025.
>
> \(c\) **Total Earnest Money.** The First Deposit and the Second Deposit shall collectively constitute the Earnest Money, totaling One Hundred Ninety-Five Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$195,000.00).
>
> \(d\) **Holding of Earnest Money.** The Earnest Money shall be held by the Escrow Agent in an interest-bearing account at a federally insured financial institution. All interest earned on the Earnest Money shall be credited to Buyer at Closing or, in the event of a termination of this Agreement, shall be disbursed to the party entitled to the Earnest Money in accordance with the terms hereof.
>
> \(e\) **Application at Closing.** At Closing, the Earnest Money, together with all accrued interest thereon, shall be applied and credited against the Purchase Price.

**Section 3.3 --- Financing**

Buyer intends to finance a portion of the Purchase Price with a first mortgage loan from Tidewater Savings Bank (loan officer: Marcus J. Pellegrino) in the principal amount of Two Million Six Hundred Forty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$2,640,000.00), at an interest rate of 6.875% per annum, fixed for seven (7) years, with a thirty (30)-year amortization schedule and a seven (7)-year balloon payment. The loan origination fee is 1.00% of the loan amount, equal to Twenty-Six Thousand Four Hundred and 00/100 Dollars (\$26,400.00).

Buyer\'s obligation to close under this Agreement is NOT contingent upon obtaining financing. The financing contingency was waived by Buyer as of June 10, 2025, and Buyer shall be obligated to proceed to Closing regardless of the status of Buyer\'s mortgage loan application.

Any fees or costs associated with Buyer\'s financing that have been paid by Buyer prior to Closing, including without limitation any appraisal fees paid directly to the Lender or its agents outside of the Closing (\"Paid Outside of Closing\" or \"POC\" items), shall be noted on the settlement statement for disclosure purposes only and shall not be included in the computation of Buyer\'s funds required at Closing. Such POC items are identified solely for the purpose of transparency and regulatory compliance and do not affect the calculation of credits, debits, or the net amount due from or to either party on the settlement statement.

**[ARTICLE 4 --- DUE DILIGENCE]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Due Diligence Period**

Buyer was afforded a due diligence period of thirty (30) days, commencing on the Effective Date (April 22, 2025) and expiring at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on May 22, 2025 (the \"Due Diligence Period\"). During the Due Diligence Period, Buyer had the right to conduct such investigations, inspections, and reviews of the Property and related documents as Buyer deemed necessary or appropriate. Buyer completed its due diligence and deposited the Second Deposit on May 22, 2025, thereby waiving all due diligence contingencies and electing to proceed with the transaction.

**Section 4.2 --- Access and Inspections**

During the Due Diligence Period, Seller granted Buyer and its agents, representatives, consultants, and contractors reasonable access to the Property for the purpose of conducting inspections, environmental assessments, surveys, engineering evaluations, and other due diligence activities. Such access was provided upon reasonable prior notice to Seller and the Property Manager, and was subject to the rights of tenants in possession. Buyer\'s pre-closing inspection rights are preserved under Section 8.3 of this Agreement.

**Section 4.3 --- Document Review**

Seller provided Buyer with copies of all material documents relating to the ownership, operation, and management of the Property, including without limitation: all leases (commercial and residential), the management agreement with Bayshore Management Co., property tax bills for the current and prior fiscal years, utility account statements, current and historical rent rolls, security deposit records, insurance policies and certificates, maintenance and repair records, capital improvement records, and all permits, licenses, and certificates of occupancy in Seller\'s possession or control.

**[ARTICLE 7 --- PRORATIONS AND ADJUSTMENTS]{.underline}**

All prorations and adjustments shall be made as of 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on the Closing Date (July 15, 2025), such that Seller shall be responsible for all income and expenses attributable to the period prior to the Closing Date, and Buyer shall be responsible for all income and expenses attributable to the Closing Date and thereafter. The day of Closing (July 15, 2025) shall be allocated to Buyer for purposes of all prorations. Unless otherwise specified herein, all prorations shall be calculated on a per diem basis using a 365-day year.

**Section 7.1 --- Real Property Tax Proration**

> \(a\) **Current Fiscal Year (July 1, 2024 -- June 30, 2025).** The annual real property tax for the current fiscal year is Fifty-Two Thousand Four Hundred Eighty and 00/100 Dollars (\$52,480.00), payable in two installments. Seller represents that the first installment in the amount of Twenty-Six Thousand Two Hundred Forty and 00/100 Dollars (\$26,240.00), due July 1, 2024, was paid on August 1, 2024. The second installment in the amount of Twenty-Six Thousand Two Hundred Forty and 00/100 Dollars (\$26,240.00), due January 1, 2025, is currently delinquent and unpaid. Seller shall be solely responsible for the payment of all delinquent property taxes, together with any and all accrued interest and penalties thereon, calculated at the rate prescribed by Connecticut law. Seller shall obtain and deliver to the Closing Agent a municipal tax and lien payoff statement from the City of Bridgeport Tax Collector\'s Office no later than five (5) business days prior to Closing. Payment of all delinquent taxes, interest, and penalties shall be made from Seller\'s proceeds at Closing or, if insufficient, by separate funds provided by Seller.
>
> \(b\) **Next Fiscal Year (July 1, 2025 -- June 30, 2026).** Because the tax for the next fiscal year has not yet been set or billed as of the date hereof, the parties agree to prorate the next fiscal year\'s taxes using the current year\'s tax amount of Fifty-Two Thousand Four Hundred Eighty and 00/100 Dollars (\$52,480.00) as the basis for estimation. Seller shall be credited for any portion of the next fiscal year\'s taxes that Seller has already paid, if any, and shall be charged for Seller\'s proportionate share of the next fiscal year\'s taxes from July 1, 2025 through July 14, 2025 (a period of fourteen (14) days). Buyer shall be responsible for the period from July 15, 2025 through June 30, 2026 (a period of three hundred fifty-one (351) days). The proration shall be calculated on a per diem basis using a 365-day year. If the actual tax bill for the fiscal year commencing July 1, 2025, when issued, differs from the estimated amount used at Closing, the parties shall reprorate in accordance with Section 7.7.

**Section 7.2 --- Rent Proration --- Collected Rents**

> \(a\) All rents actually collected by Seller for the month of Closing (July 2025) shall be prorated between Seller and Buyer based on the number of days in such month. Seller shall be entitled to rents attributable to the period prior to the Closing Date, and Buyer shall be entitled to rents attributable to the Closing Date and thereafter.
>
> \(b\) Prorations of collected rents shall be calculated on a per diem basis using the actual number of days in the month of Closing (July = 31 days). For the avoidance of doubt, Seller shall be entitled to rents for the period of July 1 through July 14 (fourteen (14) days), and Buyer shall be entitled to rents for the period of July 15 through July 31 (seventeen (17) days).
>
> \(c\) Where any proration calculation results in a fractional cent, amounts shall be rounded to the nearest cent, with any remaining balance allocated to Buyer.
>
> \(d\) The rent proration shall encompass both the Commercial Lease rent and all Residential Lease rents, calculated separately for each lease and then aggregated into a single net proration amount to appear on the settlement statement. Each party\'s share of collected rents shall be calculated on the basis of the rent amounts shown on the current rent roll attached as Exhibit B.

**Section 7.3 --- Uncollected Rents**

**No credit shall be given to Buyer for any rents that are delinquent or uncollected as of the Closing Date.**

> \(a\) Any tenant rents for the month of Closing that have not been collected by Seller as of the Closing Date shall not be prorated, and Seller shall not receive a credit, nor shall Buyer receive a credit, for such uncollected amounts. In the event that any tenant has failed to pay rent for the month of July 2025 (or any prior month) as of the Closing Date, such delinquent amounts shall be excluded entirely from the rent proration calculation on the settlement statement.
>
> \(b\) After Closing, Buyer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to collect any delinquent rents owed for periods prior to Closing, provided that Buyer shall not be obligated to commence litigation or eviction proceedings for such purpose. Any amounts collected by Buyer from tenants with outstanding balances shall first be applied to current rents owed to Buyer for the month in which payment is received, and any excess shall be remitted to Seller within thirty (30) days of receipt. Buyer shall provide Seller with an accounting of any such collections upon request.
>
> \(c\) Seller shall not have the right to commence or continue any eviction or collection proceedings against any tenant of the Property after Closing without Buyer\'s prior written consent, which consent may be withheld in Buyer\'s sole and absolute discretion.

**Section 7.4 --- Security Deposits**

> \(a\) At Closing, Seller shall transfer or credit to Buyer all security deposits held by Seller or the Property Manager under the Residential Leases and the Commercial Lease, together with any interest accrued thereon as required by applicable law. Such transfer or credit shall be reflected on the settlement statement as a debit to Seller and a credit to Buyer.
>
> \(b\) The security deposit amounts and the tenants to whom they pertain are set forth in the rent roll and security deposit schedule delivered by Seller during due diligence and attached hereto as Exhibit B. Seller represents and warrants that the amounts shown on Exhibit B are true, correct, and complete as of the Effective Date and shall be updated as of the Closing Date.
>
> \(c\) Buyer acknowledges that, from and after the Closing Date, Buyer shall assume all obligations of the landlord with respect to such security deposits, including without limitation any obligations under Connecticut General Statutes § 47a-21 (as applicable to residential security deposits), any obligations arising under the terms of the Commercial Lease with respect to the commercial security deposit, and any obligations to provide notice to tenants of the transfer of security deposits to a new landlord.
>
> \(d\) The credit to Buyer for security deposits shall appear on the settlement statement as a debit to Seller and a credit to Buyer. Seller shall instruct the Property Manager to deliver all security deposit funds to the Closing Agent or directly to Buyer on or before the Closing Date.

**Section 7.5 --- Utilities**

> \(a\) All utility charges, including water, sewer, gas, electric, and any other municipal services, shall be prorated as of the Closing Date. Where a utility billing period ends on or before the Closing Date, the entire amount of such bill shall be Seller\'s sole responsibility, and the full unpaid balance shall be credited to Buyer on the settlement statement.
>
> \(b\) Where a utility billing period straddles the Closing Date (that is, where the billing period begins before the Closing Date and ends after the Closing Date), charges for such billing period shall be prorated on a per diem basis over the number of days in the applicable billing cycle, with Seller responsible for the per diem charges attributable to the period prior to the Closing Date and Buyer responsible for the per diem charges attributable to the Closing Date and thereafter.
>
> \(c\) Seller shall provide final meter readings or the most recent utility bills for all utility accounts to the Closing Agent no later than two (2) business days prior to Closing. In the event that final meter readings are not available, the parties shall estimate utility charges based on the most recent billing period and reprorate in accordance with Section 7.7 when actual figures are available.
>
> \(d\) Any utility deposits held by utility providers in Seller\'s name shall be retained by Seller unless the parties otherwise agree in writing. Buyer shall be responsible for establishing new utility accounts in Buyer\'s name as of the Closing Date, or for causing the transfer of existing accounts, as applicable.

**Section 7.6 --- Heating Oil**

Buyer shall reimburse Seller at Closing for the value of heating oil remaining in any tank or tanks on the Property as of the date of the pre-closing inspection, calculated at Seller\'s last delivered price per gallon.

> \(a\) The quantity of heating oil remaining in the tank(s) shall be determined by gauge reading during the pre-closing inspection conducted pursuant to Section 8.3 of this Agreement. The gauge reading shall be noted in writing by both parties or their representatives.
>
> \(b\) The reimbursement amount shall appear on the settlement statement as a credit to Seller and a charge to Buyer.
>
> \(c\) Seller shall provide a copy of the most recent oil delivery receipt to the Closing Agent in order to substantiate the per-gallon price used in the calculation. In the event that no delivery receipt is available, the parties shall use the prevailing retail price for No. 2 heating oil as published by the Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection for the week in which the Closing occurs.

**Section 7.7 --- Final Proration Settlement**

If any prorations or adjustments required under this Article 7 cannot be finally determined at Closing due to the unavailability of final bills, tax assessments, or other information, such prorations shall be estimated at Closing based on the best information then available, and shall be readjusted between the parties within ninety (90) days after Closing when final figures become available. Any amounts owed by one party to the other as a result of such readjustment shall be paid within ten (10) business days following demand therefor, accompanied by reasonable supporting documentation. The obligations of the parties under this Article 7 shall survive the Closing.

**[ARTICLE 8 --- CONDITIONS, INSPECTIONS, AND ESCROWS]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Conditions to Buyer\'s Obligations**

Buyer\'s obligation to consummate the Closing is subject to the satisfaction (or waiver by Buyer in writing) of each of the following conditions on or before the Closing Date:

> \(a\) All representations and warranties of Seller set forth in Article 9 of this Agreement shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, with the same force and effect as though made on and as of the Closing Date;
>
> \(b\) The Title Company shall be unconditionally willing and committed to issue the Owner\'s Title Insurance Policy in favor of Buyer in the amount of the Purchase Price, subject only to the Permitted Exceptions;
>
> \(c\) All liens and encumbrances identified in Section 5.1 of this Agreement shall have been discharged, released, or satisfied at or prior to Closing, and evidence thereof in recordable form shall have been delivered to the Closing Agent;
>
> \(d\) Seller shall have delivered all of the closing documents and items set forth in Section 6.2 of this Agreement; and
>
> \(e\) There shall have been no material adverse change in the physical condition of the Property, nor any material adverse change in the tenant occupancy or lease status of the Property, between the Effective Date and the Closing Date, other than changes disclosed to Buyer in writing and approved by Buyer.

**Section 8.2 --- Conditions to Seller\'s Obligations**

Seller\'s obligation to consummate the Closing is subject to the satisfaction (or waiver by Seller in writing) of each of the following conditions on or before the Closing Date:

> \(a\) Buyer shall have delivered the balance of the Purchase Price, as adjusted for prorations and credits, in immediately available funds, and all closing documents set forth in Section 6.3 of this Agreement; and
>
> \(b\) All representations and warranties of Buyer set forth in Article 10 of this Agreement shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date.

**Section 8.3 --- Pre-Closing Inspection**

Buyer shall have the right to conduct a walk-through inspection of the Property no earlier than two (2) business days prior to the Closing Date and no later than one (1) day prior to the Closing Date (i.e., by July 14, 2025). The purpose of such walk-through inspection is to confirm that the Property is in substantially the same condition as of the end of the Due Diligence Period, subject to ordinary wear and tear and the items addressed by the Repair Escrow in Section 8.4. The pre-closing inspection shall include a reading of the heating oil tank gauge for purposes of the heating oil adjustment pursuant to Section 7.6. Seller shall ensure that Buyer and its representatives have reasonable access to all common areas, mechanical rooms, and vacant units during the walk-through inspection.

**Section 8.4 --- Repair Escrow**

> \(a\) Seller shall deposit the sum of Forty-Five Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$45,000.00) (the \"Repair Escrow\") into an escrow account held by the Escrow Agent (Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC) at Closing, to be applied toward the cost of roof repairs identified during Buyer\'s inspection of the Property during the Due Diligence Period. The roof repairs include, without limitation, the replacement of damaged flashing, remediation of ponding areas, and patching or replacement of deteriorated membrane sections on the flat roof portions of the building.
>
> \(b\) The Repair Escrow shall be funded solely from Seller\'s proceeds at Closing. Under no circumstances shall the Repair Escrow be funded from Buyer\'s funds or from the Purchase Price proceeds otherwise payable to Buyer.
>
> \(c\) The Repair Escrow shall be held by the Escrow Agent pursuant to a separate Repair Escrow Agreement to be executed by the parties at Closing, which Repair Escrow Agreement shall govern the terms of disbursement and return of the Repair Escrow funds. The Repair Escrow shall NOT be credited to Buyer on the settlement statement. The Repair Escrow is a third-party holdback and shall appear as a charge to Seller on the settlement statement, with funds disbursed to the Escrow Agent and held in a segregated, interest-bearing account. The Repair Escrow amount shall not be counted as part of the Purchase Price or as a credit to either party, but rather as a deduction from Seller\'s net proceeds.
>
> \(d\) Disbursement of the Repair Escrow shall be governed by the Repair Escrow Agreement. Buyer shall have twelve (12) months from the Closing Date to complete the roof repairs and submit paid invoices or other reasonable documentation of completed work to the Escrow Agent for reimbursement. Disbursement requests shall be accompanied by lien waivers from the contractor(s) performing the work.
>
> \(e\) Any unused portion of the Repair Escrow remaining after twelve (12) months from the Closing Date shall be returned to Seller (or the Estate, as applicable), together with any accrued interest thereon. Buyer shall obtain no fewer than two (2) competitive bids for the roof repair work prior to commencing such work.

**[ARTICLE 9 --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF SELLER]{.underline}**

Seller hereby represents and warrants to Buyer as follows, which representations and warranties shall be true and correct as of the Effective Date and as of the Closing Date:

**Section 9.1 --- Authority**

Claudia Whitford-Barnes is the duly appointed and acting Executrix of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, having been appointed by the Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417, and has full power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement, to perform Seller\'s obligations hereunder, and to convey the Property in accordance with the terms hereof, as authorized by the Fairfield County Probate Court and pursuant to the fiduciary powers granted under the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford.

**Section 9.2 --- Title**

The Estate holds marketable and insurable fee simple title to the Property, subject only to the liens and encumbrances described in Section 5.1 of this Agreement (all of which shall be discharged, released, or satisfied at or prior to Closing) and the Permitted Exceptions set forth in Section 5.2. The Estate has not granted any option, right of first refusal, or other right to purchase the Property to any third party.

**Section 9.3 --- Leases**

Seller represents and warrants that:

> \(a\) The Commercial Lease with Coastal Provisions Market LLC (ten-year term: January 1, 2021 through December 31, 2030; monthly base rent: Seven Thousand Two Hundred and 00/100 Dollars (\$7,200.00); security deposit: Fourteen Thousand Four Hundred and 00/100 Dollars (\$14,400.00)) is in full force and effect, and to Seller\'s knowledge, there are no defaults by landlord or tenant thereunder;
>
> \(b\) The Residential Leases for the occupied units are in full force and effect, subject to the rent delinquency status shown on the rent roll delivered to Buyer during the Due Diligence Period and attached as Exhibit B;
>
> \(c\) Unit 3C is currently vacant and is not subject to any lease or occupancy agreement; and
>
> \(d\) Copies of all leases delivered to Buyer during the Due Diligence Period are true, correct, and complete copies of the originals in Seller\'s possession.

**Section 9.4 --- Compliance with Law**

To Seller\'s knowledge, the Property is in material compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, ordinances, rules, and regulations, including without limitation zoning regulations (MU-2 Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District), building codes, fire safety codes, and environmental laws. Seller has received no written notice of any violation of any such laws or regulations.

**Section 9.5 --- Litigation and Liens**

Other than the mechanic\'s lien filed by Northbridge Construction Co., which shall be settled and released at or prior to Closing as set forth in Section 5.3(c), there is no pending or, to Seller\'s knowledge, threatened litigation, action, or proceeding affecting the Property, the Estate\'s ownership thereof, or the Estate\'s ability to convey title to the Property to Buyer.

**Section 9.6 --- Environmental**

To Seller\'s knowledge, no hazardous materials, hazardous substances, pollutants, or contaminants (as those terms are defined under applicable federal, state, or local environmental laws) have been released, stored, disposed of, or otherwise placed on, in, or under the Property in violation of applicable environmental laws. Seller has received no written notice from any governmental authority regarding any environmental contamination or remediation obligation with respect to the Property.

**Section 9.7 --- Rent Roll Accuracy**

The rent roll and security deposit schedule delivered to Buyer during the Due Diligence Period and attached hereto as Exhibit B are true, correct, and complete in all material respects as of the date delivered. Seller shall deliver an updated rent roll and security deposit schedule to Buyer and the Closing Agent no later than three (3) business days prior to the Closing Date, reflecting the status of all leases, rents, and security deposits as of such date.

**Section 9.8 --- Property Taxes**

Seller represents and warrants that:

> \(a\) The annual real property tax for the current fiscal year (July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025) is Fifty-Two Thousand Four Hundred Eighty and 00/100 Dollars (\$52,480.00);
>
> \(b\) The first installment of Twenty-Six Thousand Two Hundred Forty and 00/100 Dollars (\$26,240.00) has been paid;
>
> \(c\) The second installment of Twenty-Six Thousand Two Hundred Forty and 00/100 Dollars (\$26,240.00), due January 1, 2025, is delinquent and unpaid as of the date hereof; and
>
> \(d\) Seller shall be solely responsible for all delinquent taxes and all accrued interest and penalties thereon, calculated at the applicable statutory rate, and such amounts shall be paid at or from the proceeds of Closing.

**Section 9.9 --- Management Agreement**

The Property is currently managed by Bayshore Management Co. under a management agreement that is terminable upon the sale of the Property, subject to a termination fee of Four Thousand Five Hundred and 00/100 Dollars (\$4,500.00) as set forth in Section 11.2 of the management agreement. Seller shall be solely responsible for the payment of such termination fee and shall cause such termination to be effective as of the Closing Date.

**[ARTICLE 10 --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF BUYER]{.underline}**

Buyer hereby represents and warrants to Seller as follows, which representations and warranties shall be true and correct as of the Effective Date and as of the Closing Date:

**Section 10.1 --- Organization and Authority**

Meridian Cove Properties LLC is a Connecticut limited liability company, duly formed on January 8, 2024, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Connecticut. Elaine R. Matsuda, as Manager of Buyer, has full power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and to consummate the transaction contemplated herein on behalf of Buyer, and all necessary approvals of Buyer\'s members have been obtained.

**Section 10.2 --- Financial Capacity**

Buyer represents that it has the financial capacity to consummate the purchase of the Property, through a combination of the mortgage loan from Tidewater Savings Bank described in Section 3.3 and equity funds available to Buyer. The financing contingency was waived by Buyer as of June 10, 2025, and Buyer\'s obligation to close is not contingent upon obtaining financing.

**Section 10.3 --- Due Diligence**

Buyer acknowledges that it has completed its due diligence investigation of the Property during the Due Diligence Period and is satisfied with the condition of the Property, subject to the repair obligations addressed by the Repair Escrow set forth in Section 8.4. Buyer has had the opportunity to review all documents, records, and information provided by Seller pursuant to Article 4 and has conducted such inspections and investigations as Buyer deemed necessary.

**[ARTICLE 11 --- ASSIGNMENT OF LEASES]{.underline}**

**Section 11.1 --- Commercial Lease Assignment**

At Closing, Seller shall assign to Buyer, and Buyer shall assume, all of Seller\'s right, title, and interest in and to the Commercial Lease with Coastal Provisions Market LLC (operated by Ryan and Sara Mulgrew), pursuant to the Assignment and Assumption of Leases to be executed at Closing. From and after the Closing Date, Buyer shall assume all obligations of the landlord under the Commercial Lease and shall be entitled to all benefits thereof. The Commercial Lease has approximately five (5) years and approximately five (5) months remaining (expiring December 31, 2030).

**Section 11.2 --- Residential Lease Assignment**

At Closing, Seller shall assign to Buyer, and Buyer shall assume, all of Seller\'s right, title, and interest in and to the Residential Leases for all occupied residential units at the Property. From and after the Closing Date, Buyer shall assume all obligations of the landlord under the Residential Leases, including without limitation all obligations with respect to security deposits held under such leases in accordance with applicable law.

**Section 11.3 --- Tenant Notification**

Promptly after Closing, and in no event later than fifteen (15) days following the Closing Date, Buyer shall send written notice to all tenants of the Property advising of the change in ownership of the Property, providing new rent payment instructions (including the name and address to which future rent payments shall be directed), and identifying the name and address of the new holder of security deposits, all in compliance with applicable Connecticut law.

**[ARTICLE 12 --- CONVEYANCE TAX]{.underline}**

**Section 12.1 --- Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax**

The conveyance of the Property shall be subject to the Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax imposed under Connecticut General Statutes § 12-494, as amended. The tax shall be computed at the rate or rates applicable to the municipality in which the Property is located, as determined by the Closing Agent in accordance with applicable law.

The total conveyance tax shall be divided equally between Seller and Buyer, with each party responsible for fifty percent (50%) of the total tax due. Payment of each party\'s share shall be reflected on the settlement statement and shall be collected by the Closing Agent at Closing.

The conveyance tax return shall be prepared by the Closing Agent and shall be filed with the Town Clerk of the City of Bridgeport simultaneously with the recording of the Executor\'s Warranty Deed.

**[ARTICLE 13 --- DEFAULT AND REMEDIES]{.underline}**

**Section 13.1 --- Seller Default**

In the event that Seller fails to perform any of its material obligations under this Agreement and such failure is not cured within ten (10) days after written notice from Buyer, Buyer\'s sole and exclusive remedies shall be:

> \(a\) To bring an action for specific performance of this Agreement, compelling Seller to convey the Property in accordance with the terms hereof; or
>
> \(b\) To terminate this Agreement by written notice to Seller, in which event the Escrow Agent shall return the Earnest Money (together with all accrued interest) to Buyer, and Seller shall reimburse Buyer for Buyer\'s actual, documented, out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with this transaction, in an aggregate amount not to exceed Twenty-Five Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$25,000.00).

The remedies set forth in this Section 13.1 are mutually exclusive, and the election of one remedy shall constitute a waiver of the other.

**Section 13.2 --- Buyer Default**

In the event that Buyer fails to perform any of its material obligations under this Agreement and such failure is not cured within ten (10) days after written notice from Seller, Seller\'s sole and exclusive remedy shall be to terminate this Agreement and retain the Earnest Money (together with all accrued interest) as liquidated damages, it being agreed by the parties that Seller\'s actual damages in the event of Buyer\'s default would be difficult or impossible to ascertain with certainty, and that the Earnest Money represents a reasonable estimate of such damages.

**[ARTICLE 14 --- RISK OF LOSS]{.underline}**

**Section 14.1 --- Casualty**

Risk of loss or damage to the Property by fire, casualty, or any other cause shall remain with Seller until the Closing. If, prior to Closing, the Property suffers casualty damage, the following shall apply:

> \(a\) If the cost of repair is estimated to be One Hundred Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$100,000.00) or less, Buyer shall proceed to Closing, and Seller shall assign to Buyer all of Seller\'s rights under any applicable insurance policies and shall credit Buyer at Closing for the amount of any applicable deductible;
>
> \(b\) If the cost of repair is estimated to exceed One Hundred Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$100,000.00), Buyer may elect, by written notice to Seller within ten (10) days after learning of such casualty, either to (i) terminate this Agreement and receive a full return of the Earnest Money (together with all accrued interest), or (ii) proceed to Closing with an assignment of Seller\'s insurance proceeds and a credit for the amount of any applicable deductible.

**Section 14.2 --- Condemnation**

If, prior to Closing, condemnation proceedings are commenced or threatened against all or any material portion of the Property by any governmental authority, Buyer may elect, by written notice to Seller within ten (10) days after learning of such proceedings, either to (a) terminate this Agreement and receive a full return of the Earnest Money (together with all accrued interest), or (b) proceed to Closing with an assignment of any condemnation award or claim therefor.

**[ARTICLE 15 --- MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**Section 15.1 --- Survival**

The representations, warranties, and indemnification obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall survive the Closing for a period of twelve (12) months from the Closing Date, and no claim may be brought with respect thereto after such twelve (12)-month period. The proration and adjustment obligations set forth in Article 7 shall survive the Closing for a period of ninety (90) days for purposes of reproration and final settlement.

**Section 15.2 --- Notices**

All notices, demands, consents, approvals, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when (a) delivered personally, (b) sent by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service, or (d) sent by electronic mail (with confirmation of receipt), addressed as follows:

**If to Buyer:**

> Meridian Cove Properties LLC c/o Ridgeline Law Group PLLC 915 Chapel Street, Suite 400 New Haven, CT 06510
>
> Attn: Nora F. Kapadia, Esq.

**If to Seller:**

> Estate of Gerald T. Whitford c/o Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP 200 Atlantic Street, 14th Floor Stamford, CT 06901
>
> Attn: David H. Clement, Esq.

**If to Escrow Agent:**

> Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC 88 Field Point Road Greenwich, CT 06830
>
> Attn: Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer

Either party may change its address for notices by giving written notice of such change to the other party in the manner specified above.

**Section 15.3 --- Entire Agreement**

This Agreement, together with all exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference, constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, negotiations, understandings, representations, and warranties, whether oral or written.

**Section 15.4 --- Amendments**

This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument duly executed and delivered by both Seller and Buyer.

**Section 15.5 --- Governing Law**

This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of Connecticut, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

**Section 15.6 --- Counterparts**

This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original instrument, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same Agreement. Facsimile and electronic (PDF) signatures shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

**Section 15.7 --- Brokers**

Each party represents and warrants to the other that no broker, finder, or real estate agent was involved in or instrumental in negotiating or consummating this transaction, and that no brokerage commission, finder\'s fee, or similar compensation is due or payable to any person or entity as a result of this transaction. Each party shall indemnify and hold harmless the other party from and against any and all claims, demands, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorney fees) arising from any claim for brokerage commissions, finder\'s fees, or similar compensation asserted by any person or entity claiming to have been engaged by the indemnifying party.

**Section 15.8 --- Binding Effect**

This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, heirs, administrators, executors, personal representatives, and permitted assigns.

**Section 15.9 --- Severability**

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

**Section 15.10 --- Time of the Essence**

Time is of the essence with respect to all dates, deadlines, and time periods set forth in this Agreement.

Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC, as duly authorized issuing agent for Continental Hartleigh Title Insurance Company (hereinafter referred to as the \"Company\"), hereby commits to issue its policy or policies of title insurance, as identified in Schedule A hereof, in favor of the proposed insured(s) named in Schedule A, upon compliance with the requirements set forth in Schedule B-I and subject to the exceptions set forth in Schedule B-II. This Commitment for Title Insurance is issued in contemplation of the transaction described herein. The title insurance policy or policies will be issued only upon satisfaction of all Schedule B-I requirements and only with respect to the estate or interest in the land described in Schedule A. This Commitment is subject to the Conditions and Stipulations set forth at the end of this document, which are incorporated herein by reference and made a part of this Commitment. This Commitment shall not be valid or binding until countersigned by an authorized officer or agent of Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC and shall expire on the date set forth above unless extended by written endorsement.

**[SCHEDULE A --- TRANSACTION INFORMATION AND PROPOSED POLICIES]{.underline}**

**Section A-1: Transaction Details**

**Effective Date of Title Search:** June 16, 2025

**Proposed Closing Date:** July 15, 2025

**Property Address:** 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604

**Type of Transaction:** Purchase (Estate Sale --- Probate)

**Purchase Price:** \$3,900,000.00

**Name of Seller / Current Owner of Record:** Gerald T. Whitford (record title holder, deceased September 3, 2024). The subject property is being conveyed by the **Estate of Gerald T. Whitford**, by and through **Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix**, appointed pursuant to the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford, admitted to probate in the **Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417**. Letters Testamentary were issued to Claudia Whitford-Barnes on or about October 14, 2024.

**Vesting of Current Title:** Fee simple absolute in Gerald T. Whitford, as evidenced by that certain Warranty Deed from Harborview Associates LLC to Gerald T. Whitford, dated March 7, 2001, and recorded on **March 9, 2001**, in Volume 245, Page 312, of the Bridgeport Land Records, Office of the Town Clerk, City of Bridgeport, Connecticut. No subsequent conveyance of record has been identified prior to the death of Gerald T. Whitford.

**Section A-2: Proposed Insured --- Owner\'s Policy**
