**[PRE-CLOSING INSPECTION REPORT AND HEATING OIL TANK READING]{.underline}**

**Prepared in Connection with the Sale and Purchase of** **4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Inspection Date:**                **July 14, 2025**
  ----------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------
  **Report Date:**                    July 14, 2025

  **Prepared For:**                   Meridian Cove Properties LLC (Buyer)

                                      Ridgeline Law Group PLLC (Buyer\'s Counsel)

  **Prepared By:**                    Soundview Property Inspections LLC

                                      Craig D. Ambrose, Licensed Property Inspector

                                      CT License No. HI-2019-03841

                                      47 Seaview Avenue, Bridgeport, CT 06604

  **Reference:**                      Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025

  **Scheduled Closing Date:**         July 15, 2025
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 1: Inspection Summary and Scope]{.underline}**

This report documents a pre-closing walk-through inspection conducted on July 14, 2025, at approximately 10:00 a.m. at the property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604.

**Legal Description.** The property is identified as Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records.

**Property Type.** The subject property is a mixed-use building consisting of a ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet, currently occupied by Coastal Provisions Market LLC, and twelve residential apartments designated as Units 2A through 2F on the second floor and Units 3A through 3F on the third floor.

**Building Characteristics.** The building was originally constructed in 1998 and underwent a partial renovation in 2019. The total building area is approximately 18,400 square feet, situated on a 0.38-acre lot. The property is located within the MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District) zoning classification.

**Purpose of Inspection.** The purpose of this pre-closing inspection is to: (a) document the condition of the property immediately prior to the scheduled closing; (b) verify the completion status of any agreed-upon repairs; (c) confirm the presence and quantity of heating oil in the building\'s storage tank for the purpose of calculating a closing adjustment; and (d) identify any material changes in the condition of the property since the initial due diligence inspection.

**Persons Present During Inspection.**

> • Craig D. Ambrose, Licensed Property Inspector, Soundview Property Inspections LLC
>
> • Elaine R. Matsuda, Manager, Meridian Cove Properties LLC
>
> • Tomás Herrera, Operations Director, Bayshore Management Co. (representing the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford)

**Weather Conditions.** Clear skies, approximately 78°F, dry conditions. No precipitation in the preceding 48 hours.

**[Section 2: Exterior Condition]{.underline}**

**Building Envelope.** The exterior consists of brick masonry on the ground level and fiber-cement siding on the upper floors. Both materials were observed to be in generally acceptable condition. No new cracks, settlement patterns, or structural distress were identified since the due diligence inspection conducted in May 2025. Minor cosmetic paint peeling was observed on the third-floor window trim along the north elevation. This condition was previously noted during due diligence and does not constitute a material deficiency.

**Parking Area and Grounds.** The asphalt parking lot accommodates approximately eighteen vehicles and is in fair overall condition. Two areas of moderate cracking near the southwest corner of the lot were previously identified during the due diligence inspection period; no worsening of these conditions was observed. Landscaping is maintained and in seasonal order. No debris, unauthorized dumping, or evidence of hazardous materials were observed in the exterior areas.

**Windows and Doors.** The ground-floor commercial storefront windows are intact and in good condition. Residential windows were checked on a spot-check basis, including Units 2A, 3B, and 3D; all operated properly, with hardware and weatherstripping in functional condition. The rear service door on the east side of the building closes securely and the lock engages properly.

**Foundation.** Visible portions of the poured-concrete foundation were inspected from grade level and from within the basement. No new cracks, efflorescence, or evidence of water intrusion were observed.

**[Section 3: Roof Condition --- Repair Escrow Items]{.underline}**

**Roof Type.** The primary roof system consists of a modified bitumen flat roof over the main building area. A secondary EPDM (ethylene propylene diene monomer) membrane covers the rear mechanical room extension, which was added during the 2019 renovation.

**Condition.** The inspector confirms that the roof deficiencies identified during the initial due diligence inspection conducted in May 2025 remain **unrepaired** as of the date of this pre-closing inspection. The specific deficiencies are as follows:

> \(a\) **Blistering and Delamination.** Multiple areas of blistering and delamination are present on the modified bitumen membrane along the south-facing section of the roof. Approximately 400 square feet of membrane area is affected. The blistering is consistent with age-related deterioration and UV exposure.
>
> \(b\) **Compromised Flashing.** The flashing at the parapet wall junctions on both the east and west elevations has separated from the substrate in several locations. These gaps are potential points of water ingress during rain events and are considered a significant maintenance deficiency.
>
> \(c\) **Ponding Water Evidence.** Evidence of ponding water was observed near two roof drains located in the northwest and southeast quadrants of the main roof. Residual staining, algae growth, and minor sediment deposits indicate that water does not drain adequately from these areas, suggesting inadequate drainage slope or partially obstructed drain assemblies.
>
> \(d\) **EPDM Seam Separation.** The EPDM membrane over the mechanical room extension exhibits seam separation in two locations, each measuring approximately six to eight linear feet in length. These seam failures compromise the watertight integrity of the membrane and will require professional repair or overlay.

**Leakage Evidence.** Interior evidence of prior water infiltration was observed. Staining is present on ceiling tiles in Unit 3E, which is situated directly below the south-facing roof section, and in the third-floor common hallway adjacent to that unit. The staining appears consistent with conditions previously documented during the due diligence period. No evidence of active leaking was observed on the date of inspection, which is attributable to the dry weather conditions at the time.

**Note Regarding Repair Escrow.** Per Section 8.4 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025, the parties have agreed that the sum of Forty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$45,000.00) shall be deposited by the Seller into escrow at closing with Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC to fund the remediation of the roof deficiencies described above. As of this inspection date, the repairs have not been commenced. The inspector recommends that the Buyer obtain competitive contractor estimates promptly after closing to confirm that the escrow amount is adequate to cover the full scope of necessary roof work, including membrane replacement, flashing repair, drainage correction, and EPDM seam repair.

**Estimated Useful Life of Roof (Absent Repairs).** Based on current conditions, the inspector estimates that the modified bitumen sections have approximately one to two years of remaining useful life without intervention. The EPDM membrane over the mechanical room extension may fail sooner if the seam separations are not addressed, particularly during freeze-thaw cycles in the coming winter season.

**[Section 4: Interior Condition --- Common Areas]{.underline}**

**Lobbies and Hallways.** The first-floor lobby is in good condition, with tile flooring, painted drywall, and adequate lighting. The second- and third-floor hallways are in acceptable condition. Carpeting in the hallways shows normal wear consistent with the building\'s age and occupancy levels. Overhead lighting fixtures were tested and found to be functional throughout all common corridors.

**Stairwells.** The building has two stairwells --- a front stairwell accessed from the main lobby and a rear stairwell accessed from the east service corridor. Both stairwells are in acceptable condition. Handrails are securely fastened and continuous. Exit signage is illuminated and visible at all required locations. Treads and risers are in sound condition with no tripping hazards observed.

**Mechanical Room.** The basement mechanical room houses the building\'s primary heating and hot water systems. The oil-fired hydronic boiler was visually inspected and appears functional, with no visible leaks, unusual corrosion, or signs of malfunction. The hot water system consists of a gas-fired, 100-gallon commercial water heater that is in operable condition. The main electrical panel is labeled and accessible, with no evidence of overheating, scorching, or improper modifications.

**Laundry Room (Second Floor).** The second-floor laundry room contains two washing machines and two clothes dryers, all of which were in place and appeared operational based on a visual check. No leaks or drainage issues were noted.

**Unit 3C (Vacant).** The inspector accessed vacant Unit 3C with the cooperation of Bayshore Management Co. The unit is in broom-clean condition. No evidence of damage, pest infestation, or moisture intrusion was observed. All appliances are in place, including a refrigerator, range/oven, and dishwasher. The unit appears turnkey-ready for leasing upon the Buyer\'s acquisition of the property.

**[Section 5: Interior Condition --- Commercial Space (Ground Floor)]{.underline}**

**Tenant.** The ground-floor commercial space is occupied by Coastal Provisions Market LLC, a specialty grocery and market operated by Ryan and Sara Mulgrew.

**Observation.** The ground-floor commercial space, comprising approximately 3,200 square feet, was observed to be open and conducting normal business operations at the time of the inspection. The inspector conducted a visual walk-through of the space with the cooperation of on-site staff. Ms. Mulgrew was not present during the walk-through, but the store manager permitted access for the limited purposes of this inspection.

**Condition.** The commercial space is in good overall condition. Tenant improvements, including refrigerated display cases, shelving, and the point-of-sale area, appear well-maintained. No visible structural issues, water damage, or apparent building code violations were observed. The HVAC system serving the commercial space, which operates from a separate rooftop unit independent of the building\'s hydronic heating system, was operating normally and maintaining comfortable interior temperatures.

**Note.** The inspector did not conduct a detailed inspection of tenant-owned fixtures and equipment, which constitute the personal property of the tenant and are not being conveyed in this transaction. This report addresses only the building systems, structural elements, and landlord-owned improvements within the commercial space.

**[Section 6: Heating Oil Tank Reading]{.underline}**

**Heating System.** The building is heated by an oil-fired hydronic boiler system that provides hot water heat to the twelve residential apartments and to the common areas. (The ground-floor commercial space is served by a separate HVAC system and does not draw on the oil-fired boiler.) Heating oil for the boiler is stored in an above-ground storage tank located in the basement mechanical room.

**Tank Description.** The storage tank is a 275-gallon steel above-ground storage tank of single-wall construction. The tank is equipped with a standard float-style fuel gauge and is positioned on a level concrete pad in the mechanical room. The tank was visually inspected and appears to be in acceptable condition, with no visible corrosion, leaks, seepage, or staining on the concrete floor beneath or around the tank. The fuel supply line from the tank to the boiler is copper tubing with a shut-off valve, and no leaks were observed along the supply line.

**Gauge Reading.** At the time of inspection, on July 14, 2025, at approximately 11:15 a.m., the tank gauge indicated approximately **180 gallons** of heating oil remaining. The inspector visually confirmed the gauge reading and independently verified the approximate fill level by tapping the tank exterior at multiple levels to assess the liquid line. The tapping verification was consistent with the gauge reading of approximately 180 gallons.

**Seller\'s Last Oil Delivery Receipt.** During the inspection, Tomás Herrera of Bayshore Management Co. provided the inspector with a copy of the most recent heating oil delivery receipt for the property. The details of that receipt are as follows:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**                            **Detail**
  ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------
  **Vendor**                          Shoreline Fuel & Oil Co.

  **Delivery Date**                   June 2, 2025

  **Quantity Delivered**              125.0 gallons

  **Product**                         #2 Heating Oil

  **Price Per Gallon**                \$3.85

  **Total Charge**                    \$481.25

  **Account Holder**                  Estate of Gerald T. Whitford / Bayshore Management Co. (agent)
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A copy of the Shoreline Fuel & Oil Co. delivery receipt dated June 2, 2025, is appended to this report as **Attachment A**.

**Calculation for Settlement Purposes.** Based on the tank reading of approximately 180 gallons at the Seller\'s last-delivered price of \$3.85 per gallon, the value of the heating oil remaining in the tank is calculated as follows:

> 180 gallons × \$3.85 per gallon = **\$693.00**

**Application to Settlement Statement.** Per Section 7.6 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025, the Buyer shall reimburse the Seller at closing for heating oil remaining in the tank at the Seller\'s last purchase price per gallon. Accordingly, the amount of \$693.00 should be reflected on the settlement statement as a **credit to the Seller** and a corresponding **charge to the Buyer**.

**[Section 7: Summary of Findings and Recommendations]{.underline}**

**Overall Condition.** The property at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604, is in substantially the same condition as observed during the initial due diligence inspection period in May 2025, with the exception of normal wear and seasonal changes. No material adverse changes in the condition of the building, its systems, or the site were identified during this pre-closing walk-through.

**Items Requiring Attention at Closing:**

> 1\. **Roof Deficiencies** --- The roof deficiencies detailed in Section 3 of this report remain unrepaired and are addressed by the \$45,000.00 repair escrow to be funded by the Seller at closing under Section 8.4 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement.
>
> 2\. **Heating Oil Credit** --- Approximately 180 gallons of heating oil remain in the storage tank, valued at \$693.00 based on the Seller\'s last purchase price of \$3.85 per gallon. This amount is to be credited to the Seller at closing per Section 7.6 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement.

**Items Not Requiring Attention / Informational Only:**

> • Unit 3C remains vacant and in turnkey condition, ready for leasing.
>
> • Minor cosmetic paint peeling on the third-floor window trim (north elevation) is not a material condition and does not require action prior to closing.
>
> • Parking lot cracking near the southwest corner, previously noted during due diligence, has not worsened and is not a structural concern at this time.

**Recommendation.** Based on the observations set forth in this report, the inspector recommends proceeding with the scheduled closing. The repair escrow of \$45,000.00 adequately addresses the most significant deferred maintenance item, which is the roof system. The Buyer should prioritize obtaining competitive contractor bids for the roof work promptly after closing to confirm that the escrow funds are sufficient and to commence repairs before the onset of the fall and winter weather season.

**[Section 8: Certification]{.underline}**

I, Craig D. Ambrose, hereby certify that I personally conducted the pre-closing inspection described in this report on July 14, 2025, at the property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604. The observations, findings, and recommendations set forth herein are accurate and complete to the best of my professional knowledge and judgment. This report has been prepared solely for the benefit of the parties identified herein and their legal counsel in connection with the transaction referenced above.

Whitford was admitted to probate in this Court on or about **October 11, 2024**, and Letters Testamentary were issued to Claudia Whitford-Barnes on that same date, authorizing her to act as Executrix of the decedent\'s estate with all powers and duties prescribed by law and by the terms of said Last Will and Testament.

A probate bond in the amount of **Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$500,000.00)** was set by this Court, and said bond has been duly filed with the Court and approved in accordance with applicable statutory requirements.

As of the date of this certificate, the Executrix is currently serving in her fiduciary capacity and has not been removed, has not resigned, and has not had her authority revoked, suspended, or otherwise limited by order of this Court or by operation of law.

**[Section 2 --- Scope of Fiduciary Authority]{.underline}**

This Court further certifies that, under the terms of the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford and applicable provisions of the Connecticut General Statutes, the Executrix is authorized to:

> \(a\) Manage, control, sell, convey, lease, mortgage, exchange, or otherwise dispose of any and all real and personal property belonging to the estate, at such prices and upon such terms and conditions as the Executrix, in the exercise of her fiduciary judgment, deems to be in the best interests of the estate and its beneficiaries;
>
> \(b\) Execute deeds, contracts of sale, purchase and sale agreements, assignments, bills of sale, and all other instruments and documents necessary or appropriate to effectuate such transactions;
>
> \(c\) Retain counsel, engage real estate professionals, appraisers, accountants, property managers, and other professionals as reasonably necessary for the proper administration of the estate and the disposition of estate assets.

The Executrix is specifically authorized to sell and convey the real property known as **4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604**, more particularly described as **Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records**, without further order of this Court.

The Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford contains an independent administration provision granting the Executrix broad discretionary authority over estate assets. Accordingly, no additional court approval, order, or authorization is required for the sale, conveyance, or transfer of the above-described real property or any other estate asset.

**[Section 3 --- Certification and Authentication]{.underline}**

This certificate is issued at the request of the fiduciary for the purpose of confirming her authority to convey real property belonging to the estate. This certificate is valid as of the date issued below and may be relied upon by purchasers, title insurance companies, mortgage lenders, closing agents, and their respective counsel as evidence of the Executrix\'s appointment and authority.

This Fiduciary\'s Certificate is dated **June 25, 2025**.

**ESTATE SUMMARY --- PREPARED IN CONNECTION WITH REAL PROPERTY CONVEYANCE**

July 10, 2025

**Prepared by:** David H. Clement, Esq.

**Re:** Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417; Sale of 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604

**Addressed to:**

> Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC
>
> Attn: Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer
>
> Ridgeline Law Group PLLC
>
> Attn: Nora F. Kapadia, Esq.

**Section A --- Decedent and Estate Overview**

Gerald T. Whitford died on **September 3, 2024**, and was at the time of his death a resident of Fairfield County, Connecticut. Mr. Whitford\'s Last Will and Testament, dated March 14, 2019, was admitted to probate in the Fairfield County Probate Court under Docket No. **2024-PR-04417**. Letters Testamentary were issued to **Claudia Whitford-Barnes** on October 11, 2024, appointing her as Executrix of the estate. Ms. Whitford-Barnes is the decedent\'s daughter.

The estate is represented by this firm, Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP, with the undersigned, David H. Clement, Esq., serving as attorney of record.

The real property located at **4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604** constitutes the principal real estate asset of the estate. The property has been in the Whitford family since **2001**, when Gerald T. Whitford originally purchased it. The Executrix has determined, in the exercise of her fiduciary judgment and following appropriate appraisal, that the sale of this property is in the best interests of the estate and its beneficiaries.

**Section B --- Property Description**

**Address:** 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604

**Legal Description:** Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records.

**Property Type:** Mixed-use. The property consists of a ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet with twelve (12) residential apartment units located on the second and third floors, designated as Units 2A through 2F and Units 3A through 3F.

**Year Built:** 1998, with a partial renovation completed in 2019.

**Zoning:** MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District).

The property is subject to existing leases, including a commercial lease with **Coastal Provisions Market LLC**, the ground-floor tenant, and various residential tenancy agreements covering the upper-floor apartments. Management of the residential units has been handled by **Bayshore Management Co.** (Operations Director: Tomás Herrera) since October 2024, when the Executrix engaged the management company following the decedent\'s passing.

**Section C --- Transaction Summary**

The Executrix has entered into a Purchase and Sale Agreement dated **April 22, 2025** with **Meridian Cove Properties LLC**, a Connecticut limited liability company, for the sale of the property at a purchase price of **Three Million Nine Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,900,000.00)**.

The anticipated closing date is **July 15, 2025**. The Buyer is represented by **Nora F. Kapadia, Esq.** of **Ridgeline Law Group PLLC**. The closing is to be conducted by **Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC**, with Lorraine M. Grasso serving as Closing Officer. The Buyer\'s acquisition is being financed in part by **Tidewater Savings Bank** (Loan Officer: Marcus J. Pellegrino).

**Section D --- Existing Encumbrances and Liens**

The following is a summary of known liens and encumbrances of record against the property, all of which are to be satisfied, released, or otherwise resolved at or before closing:

> **1. First Mortgage --- Harborstone Federal Credit Union.** A mortgage originated on March 15, 2015, in the original principal amount of \$1,200,000.00, secured by the subject property. A payoff statement will be obtained from Harborstone Federal Credit Union, and this mortgage shall be satisfied in full at closing from the sale proceeds.
>
> **2. Home Equity Line of Credit --- Harborstone Federal Credit Union.** A home equity line of credit with a maximum credit limit of \$300,000.00, also secured by the subject property. A payoff statement will be obtained from Harborstone Federal Credit Union, and this line of credit shall be satisfied in full and closed at closing from the sale proceeds.
>
> **3. Mechanic\'s Lien --- Northbridge Construction Co.** A mechanic\'s lien was filed on August 12, 2024, in the amount of \$37,500.00, arising from a dispute regarding unpaid work performed in connection with the 2019 renovation of the property. A settlement has been reached between the estate and Northbridge Construction Co. for a reduced payoff amount of \$31,000.00, which shall be paid at closing. Upon receipt of payment, Northbridge Construction Co. will execute and deliver a release of lien for recording.
>
> **4. Connecticut Department of Revenue Services (CT DRS) --- Estate Tax Lien.** A precautionary estate tax lien was filed by the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services against the subject property in connection with the decedent\'s estate. The estate\'s gross taxable estate, as determined and reported on the applicable Connecticut Estate Tax Return, is below the Connecticut estate tax exemption threshold for decedents dying in 2024. Accordingly, the Connecticut estate tax obligation attributable to the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford is **\$0.00**. No payment is due to CT DRS. The lien was filed as a routine precautionary measure by the Department and does not reflect any outstanding tax obligation. A release of the estate tax lien has been obtained from CT DRS and will be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at closing to clear the lien of record. The release instrument must be recorded to satisfy title requirements, but no payoff or monetary satisfaction of any kind is required in connection with this lien.

**Section E --- Estate Tax Status --- Detailed Notation**

Under Connecticut General Statutes § 12-391 et seq., the Connecticut estate tax applies to estates with a Connecticut taxable estate exceeding the applicable exemption amount. The total gross estate of Gerald T. Whitford, as reported on the Connecticut Estate Tax Return (Form CT-706/709) filed with the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services, is below the applicable Connecticut estate tax exemption threshold for decedents dying in calendar year 2024.

**Connecticut estate tax due from the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford: \$0.00.**

The CT DRS has reviewed the estate\'s return, confirmed that no estate tax is owing, and has issued a release of the estate tax lien, designated as **CT DRS Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834**. This release must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records to clear the precautionary lien of record and satisfy the title requirements of the purchaser and its title insurer. Recording of the release is a title requirement but carries no associated payoff obligation. No funds are to be disbursed to the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services at closing.

For completeness, this firm confirms that the estate\'s federal estate tax status is not directly relevant to the title clearing for this transaction. Appropriate federal filings have been or will be timely made in accordance with applicable Internal Revenue Code requirements.

**Section F --- Representations and Enclosures**

The Executrix represents that she has full authority to sell and convey the subject property, that the estate is not subject to any pending litigation that would impair the conveyance, other than the mechanic\'s lien noted in Section D(3) above, which is being resolved by agreed settlement at closing, and that there are no other claims, disputes, or proceedings of which the Executrix is aware that would affect marketable title to the property.

The Executrix further confirms that all required probate court notices have been given in accordance with Connecticut General Statutes and applicable court rules, and that no objections to the sale of the property have been filed with the Fairfield County Probate Court.

The following documents are enclosed with this Estate Summary:

> 1\. Certified copy of Fiduciary Certificate (dated June 25, 2025)
>
> 2\. Certified copy of Letters Testamentary (issued October 11, 2024)
>
> 3\. Copy of relevant pages of the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford (provisions authorizing sale of real property)
>
> 4\. CT DRS Estate Tax Lien Release (Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834)
>
> 5\. CT DRS letter confirming \$0.00 estate tax obligation

All inquiries regarding this Estate Summary or the estate\'s interests in the transaction should be directed to the undersigned at the contact information provided below.

Respectfully submitted,

ASHFORD, CLEMENT & PAIGE LLP

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

David H. Clement, Esq. 200 Atlantic Street, 14th Floor Stamford, Connecticut 06901 Telephone: (203) 555-4100

Email: dclement@ashfordclement.com

Counsel for the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

- Water/sewer: 1,847.60 (period ends 7/14, before closing)
- Heating oil: 180 gal × 3.85 = 693.00
- POC: appraisal 4,500.00 (paid 5/5/2025)

## Lien payoffs good through 7/20/2025; per diem 1st mtg 112.67/day, HELOC ~27.93/day, tax 13.12/day"}

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build settlement-statement.docx and settlement-statement-notes.docx for the
4280 Harborview Boulevard commercial closing (July 15, 2025)."""

from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, Inches, RGBColor
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.enum.section import WD_SECTION
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def money(v):
    """Format a number as accounting currency, negatives in parentheses."""
    if v is None or v == "":
        return ""
    if isinstance(v, str):
        return v
    if v < 0:
        return f"$({abs(v):,.2f})"
    return f"${v:,.2f}"

def set_cell_bg(cell, hex_color):
    tcPr = cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    shd = OxmlElement('w:shd')
    shd.set(qn('w:val'), 'clear')
    shd.set(qn('w:color'), 'auto')
    shd.set(qn('w:fill'), hex_color)
    tcPr.append(shd)

def set_cell_margins(cell, top=40, bottom=40, left=80, right=80):
    tcPr = cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    tcMar = OxmlElement('w:tcMar')
    for tag, val in (('top', top), ('bottom', bottom), ('start', left), ('end', right)):
        node = OxmlElement(f'w:{tag}')
        node.set(qn('w:w'), str(val))
        node.set(qn('w:type'), 'dxa')
        tcMar.append(node)
    tcPr.append(tcMar)

def style_run(run, size=9, bold=False, italic=False, color=None, font='Calibri'):
    run.font.size = Pt(size)
    run.font.bold = bold
    run.font.italic = italic
    run.font.name = font
    if color:
        run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(*color)

def cell_text(cell, text, size=9, bold=False, italic=False, align='left',
              color=None, font='Calibri', valign='center'):
    cell.text = ""
    p = cell.paragraphs[0]
    p.alignment = {'left': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT,
                   'center': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER,
                   'right': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.RIGHT}[align]
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(1)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
    # support multi-line via \n
    parts = str(text).split("\n")
    for i, part in enumerate(parts):
        if i > 0:
            p.add_run().add_break()
        r = p.add_run(part)
        style_run(r, size=size, bold=bold, italic=italic, color=color, font=font)
    cell.vertical_alignment = {'center': WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.CENTER,
                               'top': WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.TOP,
                               'bottom': WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.BOTTOM}[valign]
    set_cell_margins(cell)
    return cell

def set_table_borders(table, color="999999", sz=4):
    tbl = table._tbl
    tblPr = tbl.tblPr
    borders = OxmlElement('w:tblBorders')
    for edge in ('top', 'left', 'bottom', 'right', 'insideH', 'insideV'):
        el = OxmlElement(f'w:{edge}')
        el.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
        el.set(qn('w:sz'), str(sz))
        el.set(qn('w:space'), '0')
        el.set(qn('w:color'), color)
        borders.append(el)
    tblPr.append(borders)

def set_col_widths(table, widths):
    table.autofit = False
    table.allow_autofit = False
    for row in table.rows:
        for idx, w in enumerate(widths):
            if idx < len(row.cells):
                row.cells[idx].width = Inches(w)

def add_heading(doc, text, size=12, space_before=10, space_after=4, color=(31, 56, 100)):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(space_before)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(space_after)
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    r = p.add_run(text)
    style_run(r, size=size, bold=True, color=color)
    # bottom border
    pPr = p._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    pbdr = OxmlElement('w:pBdr')
    bottom = OxmlElement('w:bottom')
    bottom.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
    bottom.set(qn('w:sz'), '6')
    bottom.set(qn('w:space'), '2')
    bottom.set(qn('w:color'), '1F3864')
    pbdr.append(bottom)
    pPr.append(pbdr)
    return p

def add_para(doc, text, size=9, bold=False, italic=False, align='left',
             space_before=2, space_after=2, color=None, indent=None):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = {'left': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT,
                   'center': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER,
                   'right': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.RIGHT,
                   'justify': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY}[align]
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(space_before)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(space_after)
    if indent:
        p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(indent)
    r = p.add_run(text)
    style_run(r, size=size, bold=bold, italic=italic, color=color)
    return p

def add_bullet(doc, text, size=9, bold_lead=None, indent=0.3):
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style='List Bullet')
    p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(indent)
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(1)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
    if bold_lead:
        r = p.add_run(bold_lead)
        style_run(r, size=size, bold=True)
        r2 = p.add_run(text)
        style_run(r2, size=size)
    else:
        r = p.add_run(text)
        style_run(r, size=size)
    return p

def shade_row(row, hex_color):
    for c in row.cells:
        set_cell_bg(c, hex_color)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Document base setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def base_document():
    doc = Document()
    style = doc.styles['Normal']
    style.font.name = 'Calibri'
    style.font.size = Pt(9)
    for section in doc.sections:
        section.top_margin = Inches(0.6)
        section.bottom_margin = Inches(0.6)
        section.left_margin = Inches(0.7)
        section.right_margin = Inches(0.7)
    return doc

# ===========================================================================
# SETTLEMENT STATEMENT
# ===========================================================================

def build_settlement_statement():
    doc = base_document()

    # ---- Title block ----
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    r = p.add_run("Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC")
    style_run(r, size=11, bold=True, color=(31, 56, 100))
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)

    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    r = p.add_run("88 Field Point Road, Greenwich, CT 06830  |  Closing Officer: Lorraine M. Grasso")
    style_run(r, size=8, italic=True, color=(89, 89, 89))
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(4)

    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    r = p.add_run("COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SETTLEMENT STATEMENT")
    style_run(r, size=14, bold=True, color=(31, 56, 100))
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)

    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    r = p.add_run("Mixed-Use Property  |  Estate / Probate Sale  |  Buyer-Financed Acquisition")
    style_run(r, size=9, italic=True, color=(89, 89, 89))
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(6)

    # ---- Transaction summary table ----
    info = [
        ("File / Commitment No.:", "TC-2025-07182", "Closing Date:", "July 15, 2025"),
        ("Property:", "4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604", "Proration Date:", "12:01 a.m. July 15, 2025"),
        ("Legal Description:", "Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, Vol. 312, Pg. 88", "Closing Day To:", "Buyer"),
        ("Seller:", "Estate of Gerald T. Whitford (Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix)", "Seller's Counsel:", "Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP (D. H. Clement, Esq.)"),
        ("Buyer:", "Meridian Cove Properties LLC (Elaine R. Matsuda, Manager)", "Buyer's Counsel:", "Ridgeline Law Group PLLC (N. F. Kapadia, Esq.)"),
        ("Lender:", "Tidewater Savings Bank (M. J. Pellegrino) — Loan No. TSB-2025-CRE-04183", "Property Manager:", "Bayshore Management Co. (T. Herrera)"),
        ("Purchase Price:", "$3,900,000.00", "Loan Amount:", "$2,640,000.00 (6.875%, 30-yr am., 7-yr balloon)"),
        ("Probate Docket:", "Fairfield County Probate Ct. No. 2024-PR-04417", "Earnest Money:", "$195,000.00 (held by Escrow Agent)"),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(info), cols=4)
    set_table_borders(t, color="BFBFBF", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [1.35, 3.05, 1.25, 2.05])
    for i, (l1, v1, l2, v2) in enumerate(info):
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[0], l1, size=8, bold=True)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[1], v1, size=8)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[2], l2, size=8, bold=True)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[3], v2, size=8)
        if i % 2 == 0:
            shade_row(t.rows[i], "F2F5FA")

    add_para(doc, "All prorations computed on a 365-day year unless otherwise noted; the day of closing (July 15, 2025) "
                  "is allocated to Buyer. Amounts are in U.S. dollars. This statement is prepared for closing purposes "
                  "and is subject to final verification of payoff figures, deposit interest, and the conveyance-tax return.",
             size=8, italic=True, color=(89, 89, 89), space_before=4, space_after=2)

    # =========================================================================
    # SECTION A — SUMMARY OF TRANSACTION
    # =========================================================================
    add_heading(doc, "SECTION A — SUMMARY OF TRANSACTION")

    rows = [
        ("ITEM", "BUYER", "SELLER", True),
        ("Contract Purchase Price", money(3900000.00), money(3900000.00), False),
        ("Heating oil reimbursement (180 gal @ $3.85)", money(693.00), money(693.00), False),
        ("TOTAL AMOUNT DUE (Buyer) / DUE TO SELLER", money(3967767.00), money(3900693.00), True),
        ("", "", "", False),
        ("LESS: Earnest money deposit (held by Escrow Agent)", money(-195000.00), "", False),
        ("LESS: Loan proceeds — Tidewater Savings Bank", money(-2640000.00), "", False),
        ("LESS: Proration credits to Buyer (rents, deposits, taxes, utilities)", money(-49202.48), "", False),
        ("TOTAL CREDITS / DEDUCTIONS", money(-2884202.48), money(-1036029.17), True),
        ("", "", "", False),
        ("CASH REQUIRED FROM BUYER AT CLOSING", money(1083564.52), "", True),
        ("NET PROCEEDS TO SELLER", "", money(2864663.83), True),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(rows), cols=3)
    set_table_borders(t, color="808080", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [5.0, 1.6, 1.6])
    for i, (label, b, s, emph) in enumerate(rows):
        if label == "" and b == "" and s == "":
            for j in range(3):
                cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[j], "", size=4)
            continue
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[0], label, size=9, bold=emph)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[1], b, size=9, bold=emph, align='right')
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[2], s, size=9, bold=emph, align='right')
        if emph:
            shade_row(t.rows[i], "DCE6F1")
    # header row shading
    shade_row(t.rows[0], "1F3864")
    for c in t.rows[0].cells:
        for p in c.paragraphs:
            for r in p.runs:
                r.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(255, 255, 255)

    add_para(doc, "Buyer proration credits total $49,202.48 = rents $12,941.95 + security deposits $32,400.00 + "
                  "next-FY property tax $2,012.93 + water/sewer $1,847.60. Seller deductions total $1,036,029.17 "
                  "(see Section C). Cash from Buyer is stated before any credit for accrued earnest-money interest "
                  "(see Notes Memo, Item 2).",
             size=8, italic=True, color=(89, 89, 89), space_before=3, space_after=2)

    # =========================================================================
    # SECTION B — BUYER'S DETAILED CHARGES & CREDITS
    # =========================================================================
    add_heading(doc, "SECTION B — BUYER'S DETAILED CHARGES AND CREDITS")

    add_para(doc, "B-1.  Charges to Buyer (Debits)", size=9, bold=True, space_before=2, space_after=2)
    buyer_debits = [
        ("Purchase Price (per PSA §3.1)", 3900000.00),
        ("Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax — Buyer 50% (est., per title commitment)", 22375.00),
        ("Loan origination fee — 1.00% of $2,640,000 (Tidewater Savings Bank)", 26400.00),
        ("Lender's title insurance premium — simultaneous issue, $2,640,000 policy", 3850.00),
        ("Title search and examination fee", 1250.00),
        ("Municipal lien search fee", 250.00),
        ("Recording fee — Executor's Warranty Deed", 113.00),
        ("Recording fee — Mortgage (Tidewater Savings Bank)", 113.00),
        ("Recording fee — Assignment of Leases", 113.00),
        ("Buyer's attorney fees — Ridgeline Law Group PLLC", 12500.00),
        ("Flood certification fee", 25.00),
        ("Tax service fee", 85.00),
        ("Heating oil reimbursement to Seller (180 gal × $3.85) — PSA §7.6", 693.00),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(buyer_debits)+2, cols=2)
    set_table_borders(t, color="BFBFBF", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [6.2, 2.0])
    cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[0], "Charge", size=8, bold=True); cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[1], "Amount", size=8, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[0], "DCE6F1")
    tot = 0.0
    for i, (label, amt) in enumerate(buyer_debits, start=1):

        tot += amt
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[0], "TOTAL CHARGES TO BUYER", size=8, bold=True)
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[1], money(tot), size=8, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[-1], "F2F5FA")

    add_para(doc, "B-2.  Credits to Buyer", size=9, bold=True, space_before=6, space_after=2)
    buyer_credits = [
        ("Earnest money deposit — First $100,000 (4/24/2025) + Second $95,000 (5/22/2025)", 195000.00),
        ("Accrued interest on earnest money — per Escrow Agent (pending confirmation)", "TBD"),
        ("Loan proceeds — Tidewater Savings Bank, Loan No. TSB-2025-CRE-04183", 2640000.00),
        ("Rent proration — Buyer's share of July 2025 collected rents (PSA §7.2)", 12941.95),
        ("Security deposits transferred — 11 residential + 1 commercial (PSA §7.4)", 32400.00),
        ("Property tax proration — Seller's share, next FY 7/1–7/14/2025 (PSA §7.1(b))", 2012.93),
        ("Water/sewer proration — billing period ending 7/14/2025 (PSA §7.5(a))", 1847.60),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(buyer_credits)+2, cols=2)
    set_table_borders(t, color="BFBFBF", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [6.2, 2.0])
    cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[0], "Credit", size=8, bold=True); cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[1], "Amount", size=8, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[0], "DCE6F1")
    tot = 0.0
    for i, (label, amt) in enumerate(buyer_credits, start=1):
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[0], label, size=8)
        if amt == "TBD":
            cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[1], "TBD", size=8, italic=True, align='right', color=(192, 0, 0))
        else:

            tot += amt
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[0], "TOTAL CREDITS TO BUYER (excl. pending interest)", size=8, bold=True)
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[1], money(tot), size=8, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[-1], "F2F5FA")

    add_para(doc, "B-3.  Cash Required from Buyer at Closing", size=9, bold=True, space_before=6, space_after=2)
    t = doc.add_table(rows=3, cols=2)

    cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[0], "Total charges to Buyer", size=9); cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[1], money(3967767.00), size=9, align='right')
    cell_text(t.rows[1].cells[0], "Less: total credits to Buyer (excl. pending interest)", size=9); cell_text(t.rows[1].cells[1], money(-2884202.48), size=9, align='right')

    # =========================================================================

    # =========================================================================

    add_para(doc, "C-2.  Deductions from Seller's Proceeds (Debits)", size=9, bold=True, space_before=6, space_after=2)
    seller_debits = [

        ("Payoff — Harborstone FCU HELOC (Loan 2018-HEL-003291; good thru 7/20/2025)", 148219.56),
        ("Settlement — Northbridge Construction Co. mechanic's lien (agreed payoff)", 31000.00),

        ("Owner's title insurance premium — $3,900,000 policy (PSA §5.4(a))", 8275.00),
        ("Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax — Seller 50% (est., per title commitment)", 22375.00),

        ("Management agreement termination fee — Bayshore Management Co. (PSA §6.4)", 4500.00),

        ("Recording fee — release of Harborstone first mortgage", 73.00),
        ("Recording fee — release of Harborstone HELOC", 73.00),
        ("Recording fee — release of Northbridge mechanic's lien", 73.00),
        ("Recording fee — release of CT DRS estate tax lien (no payoff; release only)", 73.00),
        ("Probate Court Certificate (PSA §6.2(e))", 150.00),
        ("Property tax proration — Seller's share, next FY 7/1–7/14/2025 (PSA §7.1(b))", 2012.93),
        ("Rent proration — Buyer's share of July 2025 collected rents (PSA §7.2)", 12941.95),
        ("Security deposits transferred to Buyer — 11 residential + 1 commercial (PSA §7.4)", 32400.00),
        ("Water/sewer proration — billing period ending 7/14/2025 (PSA §7.5(a))", 1847.60),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(seller_debits)+2, cols=2)
    set_table_borders(t, color="BFBFBF", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [6.2, 2.0])
    cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[0], "Deduction", size=8, bold=True); cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[1], "Amount", size=8, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[0], "DCE6F1")
    tot = 0.0
    for i, (label, amt) in enumerate(seller_debits, start=1):
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[0], label, size=8)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[1], money(amt), size=8, align='right')
        tot += amt
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[0], "TOTAL DEDUCTIONS FROM SELLER", size=8, bold=True)
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[1], money(tot), size=8, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[-1], "F2F5FA")

    add_para(doc, "C-3.  Net Proceeds to Seller", size=9, bold=True, space_before=6, space_after=2)
    t = doc.add_table(rows=3, cols=2)
    set_table_borders(t, color="808080", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [6.2, 2.0])
    cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[0], "Total due to Seller", size=9); cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[1], money(3900693.00), size=9, align='right')
    cell_text(t.rows[1].cells[0], "Less: total deductions from Seller", size=9); cell_text(t.rows[1].cells[1], money(-1036029.17), size=9, align='right')
    cell_text(t.rows[2].cells[0], "NET PROCEEDS TO SELLER (by wire, per Seller instructions)", size=9, bold=True)
    cell_text(t.rows[2].cells[1], money(2864663.83), size=9, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[2], "DCE6F1")

    doc.add_page_break()

    # =========================================================================
    # SECTION D — PRORATION SCHEDULES
    # =========================================================================
    add_heading(doc, "SECTION D — PRORATION SCHEDULES")

             size=8, space_before=10, color=(89,89,89))

    add_para(doc, "Prepared by the Closing Agent for the transaction described above. This settlement statement is subject to "
                  "final verification and the reconciliation of items flagged in the accompanying Notes Memo. Proration and "
                  "adjustment obligations survive closing for 90 days per PSA §7.7 and §15.1.",
             size=7.5, italic=True, color=(89,89,89), space_before=8)

    out = "output/settlement-statement.docx"
    doc.save(out)
    print(f"Saved {out}")
    return out

# ===========================================================================
# NOTES MEMO
# ===========================================================================

def build_notes_memo():
    doc = base_document()

    # Header
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    r = p.add_run("CLOSING NOTES MEMORANDUM")
    style_run(r, size=14, bold=True, color=(31,56,100))
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)

    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    r = p.add_run("Discrepancies, Assumptions & Items Pending Confirmation")
    style_run(r, size=10, italic=True, color=(89,89,89))
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(6)

    # Memo header table
    hdr = [
        ("TO:", "Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer — Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC"),
        ("FROM:", "Settlement Statement Preparation (pre-closing review)"),
        ("DATE:", "July 14, 2025"),
        ("RE:", "Settlement statement for sale of 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604"),
        ("CLOSING:", "July 15, 2025  |  Seller: Estate of Gerald T. Whitford  |  Buyer: Meridian Cove Properties LLC"),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(hdr), cols=2)
    set_table_borders(t, color="BFBFBF", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [0.9, 7.3])
    for i,(a,b) in enumerate(hdr):
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[0], a, size=9, bold=True)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[1], b, size=9)

    add_para(doc, "This memorandum accompanies the settlement statement and identifies (i) discrepancies among the closing "
                  "documents, (ii) assumptions made in preparing the statement, and (iii) items that remain pending and must "
                  "be confirmed before or at closing. Each item is keyed to the relevant contract section or source document. "
                  "No item below prevents closing on July 15, 2025, provided the pending confirmations are obtained; several "
                  "are subject to the 90-day reproration/survival window in PSA §7.7.",
             size=9, space_before=6, space_after=4, align='justify')

    # ---- DISCREPANCIES ----
    add_heading(doc, "PART 1 — DISCREPANCIES AMONG CLOSING DOCUMENTS")

    discrepancies = [
        ("Title commitment number.",
         "The PSA (§5.1) references Title Commitment No. PCT-2025-08814, while the actual title commitment issued by "
         "Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC bears Commitment No. TC-2025-07182 (effective June 18, 2025). The commitment "
         "number cited in the contract does not match the commitment in the file. Confirm the correct commitment number "
         "and update the closing file / policy references accordingly."),
        ("Title insurer name.",
         "The title commitment header names 'Continental Fididelity Title Insurance Company,' while the body and "
         "Conditions & Stipulations refer to 'Continental Hartleigh Title Insurance Company.' The issuing agent is "
         "Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC in both. Clarify the correct underwriter name for the owner's and lender's "
         "policies before issuance."),
        ("Mechanic's lien recording reference.",
         "The title commitment (Schedule B-I, Requirement 6) states the Northbridge Construction Co. mechanic's lien was "
         "recorded at Volume 1398, Page 77 of the Bridgeport Land Records. The Northbridge settlement letter and the "
         "payoff-letter summary state the lien was recorded at Volume 1064, Page 517. These recording references conflict. "
         "Verify against the land records and ensure the release is recorded against the correct instrument."),
        ("First-mortgage and HELOC recording references.",
         "The title commitment records the Harborstone first mortgage at Volume 1087, Page 445 and the HELOC at Volume "
         "1204, Page 218. The payoff letters / summary table cite the first mortgage at 'Volume 312, Page 88' (which is "
         "in fact the plat reference for the property) and the HELOC at Volume 487, Page 112. The first-mortgage citation "
         "in the payoff package appears erroneous. Confirm the correct recording references before recording releases."),
        ("Party address / telephone inconsistencies.",
         "Contact information for the closing parties varies across documents: Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC is shown at "
         "88 Field Point Road, Greenwich (PSA/title commitment) but at 3100 Main Street, Bridgeport and 1200 Post Road "
         "East, Westport in the payoff and tax letters; Ridgeline Law Group PLLC is at 915 Chapel Street, New Haven (PSA) "
         "but 55 Railroad Avenue, Greenwich (payoff cover); and Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP's telephone appears as "
         "(203) 325-6600 (PSA), (203) 547-8100 (payoff letters), and (203) 555-4100 (probate certificate). These are "
         "clerical/template inconsistencies that do not affect the financial reconciliation, but the correct addresses "
         "and wire instructions must be verified before disbursement."),
        ("Lender LTV narrative.",
         "The commitment letter states the loan-to-value ratio is 'approximately 67.7%' based on the appraised value of "
         "$3,920,000, but then computes $2,640,000 ÷ $3,920,000 = 67.35% (which rounds to 67.4%, not 67.7%). The 67.7% "
         "figure actually corresponds to the loan divided by the purchase price ($2,640,000 ÷ $3,900,000 = 67.69%). This "
         "is a narrative inconsistency in the commitment only; it does not affect any settlement-statement figure."),
    ]
    for i,(title, body) in enumerate(discrepancies, start=1):
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(4); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
        p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.25)
        r = p.add_run(f"1.{i}  "); style_run(r, size=9, bold=True, color=(192,0,0))
        r = p.add_run(title); style_run(r, size=9, bold=True)
        p2 = doc.add_paragraph()
        p2.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.5)
        p2.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        p2.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY
        r = p2.add_run(body); style_run(r, size=9)

    # ---- ASSUMPTIONS ----
    add_heading(doc, "PART 2 — ASSUMPTIONS MADE IN PREPARING THE STATEMENT")

    assumptions = [
        ("Conveyance tax is an estimate.",
         "The Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax is reflected at $44,750.00 total ($22,375.00 each), per the title "
         "commitment's Schedule C estimate, which states it is 'calculated using the tiered rate structure applicable to "
         "the City of Bridgeport' and that 'the actual amount due will be confirmed upon preparation and filing of Form "
         "OP-236.' The exact state/municipal rate split is not specified in any document. The final figure will be "
         "confirmed when the conveyance-tax return is prepared; any difference will be adjusted 50/50 between the parties "
         "per PSA §12.1."),
        ("Next-fiscal-year property tax is estimated.",
         "The FY2025-2026 (July 1, 2025-June 30, 2026) tax has not been set or billed. Per PSA §7.1(b), the current-year "
         "levy of $52,480.00 is used as the estimate, prorated at $143.7808/day over 365 days (Seller 14 days = $2,012.93; "
         "Buyer 351 days = $50,467.07). The actual bill may differ; the parties must reprorate within 90 days after closing "
         "per PSA §7.7."),
        ("No prepaid loan interest and no escrow impounds at closing.",
         "The lender commitment lists no prepaid interest and no tax/insurance escrow impound reserves among the closing "
         "charges, and the first monthly payment is due August 15, 2025 (covering the period from closing). The statement "
         "accordingly assumes $0 prepaid interest and $0 impound reserves. If Tidewater Savings Bank requires prepaid "
         "interest (July 15-31) or initial impound deposits for taxes and insurance, additional Buyer charges will apply "
         "and the cash-to-close must be revised."),
        ("Rent proration uses the per-lease method.",
         "Per PSA §7.2(d), collected rents are prorated separately for each lease and then aggregated. This yields Buyer's "
         "share = $12,941.95 (Seller's = $10,658.05). A single aggregate calculation ($23,600.00 × 17/31) would yield "
         "$12,941.94 — a $0.01 difference. The statement uses the per-lease method as required and allocates the residual "
         "cent to Buyer per §7.2(c)."),
        ("Water/sewer treated as a Buyer credit per the PSA.",
         "The water/sewer billing period (May 15-July 14, 2025) ends before closing, so PSA §7.5(a) directs that the full "
         "unpaid balance ($1,847.60) be Seller's responsibility and 'credited to Buyer.' The statement therefore debits "
         "Seller and credits Buyer $1,847.60, and pays only the delinquent-tax portion ($28,602.80) to the City at closing. "
         "See Part 3, Item 6, for the related disbursement-split caution."),
        ("Repair escrow is a Seller holdback, not a Buyer credit.",
         "Per PSA §8.4(c), the $45,000.00 repair escrow is funded solely from Seller's proceeds, held by the Escrow Agent "
         "under a separate Repair Escrow Agreement, is NOT credited to Buyer, and is NOT part of the Purchase Price. It "
         "appears only as a deduction from Seller's net proceeds. The roof deficiencies remain unrepaired as of the "
         "July 14, 2025 pre-closing inspection (see Part 3, Item 9)."),
        ("Heating-oil value based on inspection gauge reading.",
         "The $693.00 heating-oil credit (180 gallons × $3.85/gal.) is based on the tank-gauge reading at the July 14, "
         "2025 pre-closing inspection and the Seller's last delivery receipt (Shoreline Fuel & Oil Co., June 2, 2025). "
         "The gauge reading is approximate ('approximately 180 gallons'); the parties agreed in writing to this figure "
         "at inspection per PSA §7.6(a)."),
        ("Closing occurs on or before July 20, 2025.",
         "All lien payoffs (Harborstone first mortgage $687,412.33; HELOC $148,219.56) and the delinquent-tax payoff "
         "($28,602.80) are good through July 20, 2025. The statement assumes closing on the scheduled date of July 15, "
         "2025, so no per-diem adjustments are applied. If closing is delayed beyond July 20, 2025, per-diem interest "
         "applies (first mortgage $112.67/day; HELOC ~$27.93/day; delinquent tax $13.12/day) and updated payoff "
         "statements must be obtained."),
        ("No brokerage commission.",
         "Per PSA §15.7, no broker, finder, or real estate agent was involved and no commission is due. No commission line "
         "is included on the statement."),
        ("Each party bears its own attorney fees.",
         "Per PSA §6.4, each party is responsible for its own legal fees (Buyer $12,500.00; Seller $11,000.00), reflected "
         "as charges to the respective party."),
    ]
    for i,(title, body) in enumerate(assumptions, start=1):
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(4); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
        p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.25)
        r = p.add_run(f"2.{i}  "); style_run(r, size=9, bold=True, color=(31,56,100))
        r = p.add_run(title); style_run(r, size=9, bold=True)
        p2 = doc.add_paragraph()
        p2.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.5)
        p2.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        p2.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY
        r = p2.add_run(body); style_run(r, size=9)

    # ---- PENDING ----
    add_heading(doc, "PART 3 — ITEMS PENDING CONFIRMATION BEFORE / AT CLOSING")

    pending = [
        ("Earnest-money interest (affects Buyer cash-to-close).",
         "PSA §3.2(d) requires that interest earned on the $195,000.00 earnest money be credited to Buyer at closing. The "
         "accrued interest amount is not stated in any delivered document. The statement reflects the $195,000.00 principal "
         "only; the Escrow Agent must provide the interest-bearing-account statement so the interest can be added as a "
         "Buyer credit, which will reduce the Buyer's cash required at closing dollar-for-dollar."),
        ("Security-deposit interest (affects Seller debit / Buyer credit).",
         "PSA §7.4 requires transfer of security deposits 'together with any interest accrued thereon as required by "
         "applicable law.' Connecticut General Statutes §47a-21 requires interest on residential security deposits. The "
         "delivered schedule shows principal only ($32,400.00); accrued residential-deposit interest is not quantified. "
         "Bayshore Management Co. / the Escrow Agent must confirm the interest amount, which will increase the Seller "
         "debit and Buyer credit equally."),
        ("Conveyance-tax final figure.",
         "Confirm the exact Connecticut state and Bridgeport municipal conveyance-tax amounts upon preparation of Form "
         "OP-236 (see Assumption 2.1). Adjust 50/50 if the final figure differs from the $44,750.00 estimate."),
        ("Water/sewer disbursement split.",
         "The City of Bridgeport payoff statement (TLC-2025-04892) bundles the water/sewer balance ($1,847.60) with the "
         "delinquent taxes into a single 'Total Due to City of Bridgeport' of $30,450.40. The statement follows the PSA "
         "and pays only the tax portion ($28,602.80) to the City, crediting the water/sewer to Buyer. The closing agent "
         "must confirm the split so the water/sewer is not double-paid. If the agent instead pays the City the full "
         "$30,450.40 from Seller's proceeds, the $1,847.60 Buyer credit must be removed (no change to Seller's net; "
         "Buyer's cash-to-close increases by $1,847.60)."),
        ("Uncollected rent — Unit 2E ($1,600.00).",
         "Unit 2E's July 2025 rent is delinquent. Per PSA §7.3, no credit is given to either party for uncollected rents, "
         "and the $1,600.00 is excluded from the rent proration. Post-closing, Buyer may collect delinquent rents and, per "
         "§7.3(b), remit any excess to Seller within 30 days. Buyer assumes the collection risk with no closing credit. "
         "Confirm the updated rent roll delivered within 3 business days of closing (PSA §9.7) reflects the same status."),
        ("CT DRS estate-tax-lien release recording.",
         "No payoff is due on the CT DRS estate-tax lien (estate below the exemption threshold; $0.00 tax). Release "
         "Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834 has been obtained and must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at Seller's "
         "expense ($73.00 recording fee included). Confirm the release is delivered in recordable form and recorded."),
        ("Fiduciary authority / probate certificate currency.",
         "The Fiduciary's Certificate is dated June 25, 2025 (within 60 days of closing), satisfying the title requirement. "
         "Confirm a certified copy is recorded and that the Probate Court Certificate ($150.00) is obtained. The "
         "Executrix's authority to convey without further court order is confirmed in the certificate."),
        ("Management-agreement termination and deposit transfer.",
         "The Bayshore Management Co. agreement is terminated effective July 15, 2025 (termination fee $4,500.00 from "
         "Seller's proceeds). Confirm Bayshore transfers all security deposits (with accrued interest) and records/keys "
         "to the Escrow Agent or Buyer on or before closing, and delivers a final accounting within 15 days (§11.3)."),
        ("Repair escrow — roof condition and adequacy.",
         "The July 14, 2025 pre-closing inspection confirms the roof deficiencies (blistering/delamination, compromised "
         "flashing, ponding water, EPDM seam separation) remain UNREPAIRED. The $45,000.00 escrow funds these repairs. "
         "The inspector estimates 1-2 years of remaining roof life without intervention and recommends Buyer obtain "
         "competitive bids promptly after closing to confirm the escrow is sufficient. Per §8.4(d), Buyer has 12 months "
         "to complete repairs and submit paid invoices; unused balances revert to Seller with interest."),
        ("Lender-required third-party costs not quantified.",
         "The lender commitment requires an ALTA/NSPS survey, a Phase I Environmental Site Assessment, a Subordination "
         "Non-Disturbance and Attornment Agreement (SNDA) with the commercial tenant, property insurance ($3,900,000 RCV), "
         "and entity documentation. No costs for these items are stated in any delivered document, and none are reflected "
         "on the settlement statement. Confirm whether any of these are payable at closing (e.g., survey, SNDA fees) and "
         "add as Buyer charges if so; the appraisal ($4,500.00) is already paid POC."),
        ("Final payoff figures and wire instructions.",
         "Re-verify each payoff amount and wire instruction against the original payoff letters immediately before "
         "disbursement (Harborstone first mortgage, Harborstone HELOC, Northbridge mechanic's lien, City of Bridgeport). "
         "All are good through July 20, 2025. Confirm the Seller's wire instructions for net proceeds through Seller's "
         "counsel to guard against wire-fraud risk."),
    ]
    for i,(title, body) in enumerate(pending, start=1):
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(4); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
        p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.25)
        r = p.add_run(f"3.{i}  "); style_run(r, size=9, bold=True, color=(192,90,0))
        r = p.add_run(title); style_run(r, size=9, bold=True)
        p2 = doc.add_paragraph()
        p2.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.5)
        p2.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        p2.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY
        r = p2.add_run(body); style_run(r, size=9)

    # ---- Summary of financial impact ----
    add_heading(doc, "PART 4 — SUMMARY OF FINANCIAL IMPACT OF PENDING ITEMS")
    impact = [
        ("Earnest-money interest (Item 3.1)", "Reduces Buyer cash-to-close", "Dollar-for-dollar; amount TBD by Escrow Agent"),
        ("Security-deposit interest (Item 3.2)", "Increases Seller debit / Buyer credit equally", "Net neutral to cash-to-close; amount TBD"),
        ("Conveyance-tax final (Item 3.3)", "Adjusts both parties 50/50", "Currently estimated $22,375.00 each"),
        ("Water/sewer split (Item 3.4)", "May shift $1,847.60 between Buyer credit and City payment", "No change to Seller net"),
        ("Lender third-party costs (Item 3.10)", "May add Buyer charges if payable at closing", "Not currently reflected"),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(impact)+1, cols=3)
    set_table_borders(t, color="BFBFBF", sz=4)
    set_col_widths(t, [2.6, 2.9, 2.7])
    for j,h in enumerate(["Pending Item","Effect on Statement","Magnitude / Note"]):
        cell_text(t.rows[0].cells[j], h, size=8, bold=True)
    shade_row(t.rows[0], "DCE6F1")
    for i,(a,b,c) in enumerate(impact, start=1):
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[0], a, size=8)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[1], b, size=8)
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[2], c, size=8, italic=True, color=(89,89,89))

    add_para(doc, "Bottom line: The settlement statement balances to the penny at $3,918,564.52 in / $3,918,564.52 out, "
                  "yielding cash required from Buyer of $1,083,564.52 and net proceeds to Seller of $2,864,663.83, exclusive "
                  "of the pending earnest-money interest credit. None of the flagged items is expected to be material to the "
                  "overall reconciliation; the largest open variable is the conveyance-tax finalization. Closing may proceed "
                  "on July 15, 2025 subject to obtaining the confirmations listed in Part 3.",
             size=9, space_before=6, space_after=4, align='justify')

    add_para(doc, "Prepared as a pre-closing review aid. This memorandum does not modify the Purchase and Sale Agreement "
                  "or any closing document and is subject to the terms of those controlling instruments.",
             size=8, italic=True, color=(89,89,89), space_before=6)

    out = "output/settlement-statement-notes.docx"
    doc.save(out)
    print(f"Saved {out}")
    return out

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if __name__ == "__main__":
    build_settlement_statement()
    build_notes_memo()
    print("Done.")", "file_path": "build_settlement.py"}

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build settlement-statement.docx and settlement-statement-notes.docx for the
4280 Harborview Boulevard commercial closing (July 15, 2025)."""

from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, Inches, RGBColor
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.enum.section import WD_SECTION
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def money(v):
    """Format a number as accounting currency, negatives in parentheses."""
    if v is None or v == "":
        return ""
    if isinstance(v, str):
        return v
    if v < 0:
        return f"$({abs(v):,.2f})"
    return f"${v:,.2f}"

def set_cell_bg(cell, hex_color):
    tcPr = cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    shd = OxmlElement('w:shd')
    shd.set(qn('w:val'), 'clear')
    shd.set(qn('w:color'), 'auto')
    shd.set(qn('w:fill'), hex_color)
    tcPr.append(shd)

def set_cell_margins(cell, top=40, bottom=40, left=80, right=80):
    tcPr = cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    tcMar = OxmlElement('w:tcMar')
    for tag, val in (('top', top), ('bottom', bottom), ('start', left), ('end', right)):
        node = OxmlElement(f'w:{tag}')
        node.set(qn('w:w'), str(val))
        node.set(qn('w:type'), 'dxa')
        tcMar.append(node)
    tcPr.append(tcMar)

def style_run(run, size=9, bold=False, italic=False, color=None, font='Calibri'):
    run.font.size = Pt(size)
    run.font.bold = bold
    run.font.italic = italic
    run.font.name = font
    if color:
        run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(*color)

def cell_text(cell, text, size=9, bold=False, italic=False, align='left',
              color=None, font='Calibri', valign='center'):
    cell.text = ""
    p = cell.paragraphs[0]
    p.alignment = {'left': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT,
                   'center': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER,
                   'right': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.RIGHT}[align]
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(1)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
    # support multi-line via \n
    parts = str(text).split("\n")
    for i, part in enumerate(parts):
        if i > 0:
            p.add_run().add_break()
        r = p.add_run(part)
        style_run(r, size=size, bold=bold, italic=italic, color=color, font=font)
    cell.vertical_alignment = {'center': WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.CENTER,
                               'top': WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.TOP,
                               'bottom': WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL.BOTTOM}[valign]
    set_cell_margins(cell)
    return cell

def set_table_borders(table, color="999999", sz=4):
    tbl = table._tbl
    tblPr = tbl.tblPr
    borders = OxmlElement('w:tblBorders')
    for edge in ('top', 'left', 'bottom', 'right', 'insideH', 'insideV'):
        el = OxmlElement(f'w:{edge}')
        el.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
        el.set(qn('w:sz'), str(sz))
        el.set(qn('w:space'), '0')
        el.set(qn('w:color'), color)
        borders.append(el)
    tblPr.append(borders)

def set_col_widths(table, widths):
    table.autofit = False
    table.allow_autofit = False
    for row in table.rows:
        for idx, w in enumerate(widths):
            if idx < len(row.cells):
                row.cells[idx].width = Inches(w)

def add_heading(doc, text, size=12, space_before=10, space_after=4, color=(31, 56, 100)):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(space_before)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(space_after)
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    r = p.add_run(text)
    style_run(r, size=size, bold=True, color=color)
    # bottom border
    pPr = p._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    pbdr = OxmlElement('w:pBdr')
    bottom = OxmlElement('w:bottom')
    bottom.set(qn('w:val'), 'single')
    bottom.set(qn('w:sz'), '6')
    bottom.set(qn('w:space'), '2')
    bottom.set(qn('w:color'), '1F3864')
    pbdr.append(bottom)
    pPr.append(pbdr)
    return p

def add_para(doc, text, size=9, bold=False, italic=False, align='left',
             space_before=2, space_after=2, color=None, indent=None):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = {'left': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT,
                   'center': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER,
                   'right': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.RIGHT,
                   'justify': WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY}[align]
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(space_before)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(space_after)
    if indent:
        p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(indent)
    r = p.add_run(text)
    style_run(r, size=size, bold=bold, italic=italic, color=color)
    return p

def add_bullet(doc, text, size=9, bold_lead=None, indent=0.3):
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style='List Bullet')
    p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(indent)
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(1)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(1)
    if bold_lead:
        r = p.add_run(bold_lead)
        style_run(r, size=size, bold=True)
        r2 = p.add_run(text)
        style_run(r2, size=size)
    else:
        r = p.add_run(text)
        style_run(r, size=size)
    return p

def shade_row(row, hex_color):
    for c in row.cells:
        set_cell_bg(c, hex_color)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Document base setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def base_document():
    doc = Document()
    style = doc.styles['Normal']
    style.font.name = 'Calibri'
    style.font.size = Pt(9)
    for section in doc.sections:
        section.top_margin = Inches(0.6)
        section.bottom_margin = Inches(0.6)
        section.left_margin = Inches(0.7)
        section.right_margin = Inches(0.7)
    return doc

# ===========================================================================

        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[4], money(coll), size=7.5, align='right')
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[5], money(s), size=7.5, align='right')
        cell_text(t.rows[i].cells[6], money(b), size=7.5, align='right')
        tot_rent += coll; tot_sell += s; tot_buy += b
        if st in ("UNPAID","VACANT"):
            shade_row(t.rows[i], "FCE4E4")
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[0], "TOTAL", size=7.5, bold=True, align='center')
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[1], "Collected July 2025 rents", size=7.5, bold=True)
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[2], "", size=7.5)
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[3], "", size=7.5)
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[4], money(tot_rent), size=7.5, bold=True, align='right')
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[5], money(tot_sell), size=7.5, bold=True, align='right')
    cell_text(t.rows[-1].cells[6], money(tot_buy), size=7.5, bold=True, align='right')
    shade_row(t.rows[-1], "F2F5FA")

    add_para(doc, "Net rent proration: Seller collected $23,600.00; Buyer's share $12,941.95 → Debit Seller / Credit Buyer $12,941.95. "
                  "Unit 2E ($1,600.00 delinquent) and Unit 3C (vacant) are excluded — no credit to either party (PSA §7.3). "
                  "Note: an aggregate calculation ($23,600 × 17/31) yields $12,941.94, a $0.01 difference resolved in Buyer's favor "
                  "per §7.2(c); the per-lease method above is used as required by §7.2(d).",
             size=8, italic=True, color=(89,89,89), space_before=2, space_after=2)

    # ---- D-3 Security Deposits ----

    dep_rows = [
